races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Spectacular Diver
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling purchase who is half-brother to a 5f winner (including 2yo); showed some promise with keeping-on second of four at Pontefract (5f, good) where the winner was the only runner who had the advantage of experience; the same attribute now applies to him.
runners.comment: Showed some promise with second of four at Pontefract; the only runner with experience

runners.name: Cut A Dash
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner half-brother to Danish 7.7f/1m1f winner Foretell; dam Group-placed 6f AW scorer (RPR 103), sister to 5.5f/6f Listed winner Lethal Promise, out of US 5.5f/6f winner; major stable landed this race with a newcomer 12 months ago; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Starspangledbanner colt; major stable landed this race with a newcomer 12 months ago

runners.name: Varzi
runners.spotlight: Sold for 170,000euros as a foal and a yearling; Havana Grey half-brother to Australian 1m winner Killenaule, out of 1m scorer (including Italian Listed; RPR 93); wears tongue-tie on debut; the eye-catcher in terms of auction details and he's in excellent hands to boot.
runners.comment: Sold for 170,000euros as a foal  and a yearling; by Havana Grey; in excellent hands

runners.name: Courseyoudo
runners.spotlight: 125,000euros yearling, £70,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Mehmas half-brother to French winners Star Of Brittany (6.5f AW 2yo) and Wittelsbach (1m AW 2yo); dam French Group-placed 6f/7f scorer (including 2yo; 93); first 2yo runner for his predominantly jumps yard; market instructive.
runners.comment: £70,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; first 2yo runner for his predominantly jumps yard; market helpful

runners.name: Dubai Champion
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros foal, 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Persian Force half-brother to winners Mansa Musa (5.5f/6f including 2yo/US Listed; RPR 113) and Galileo Noir (1m1f; 77); dam 6f AW scorer (74); yard's strike-rate with 2yos is only modest; others preferred.
runners.comment: 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; yard's strike-rate with 2yos is only modest; others preferred
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Fantasy Force
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros foal, £56,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Persian Force half-brother to Polish 6f 2yo winner Blue Marina; dam 1m AW scorer (RPR 65), half-sister to two Listed winners (5f/1m), out of Group-placed 7f 2yo winner; has precocious signs in his pedigree.
runners.comment: £56,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force; has precocious signs in his pedigree

races.race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Fireblade
runners.spotlight: Did well last season, including a win over 1m4f in April but he was best later on at about 2m (including at York); reappearance this term was pretty encouraging, considering it was over just 1m2f, and he looks a serious each-way contender.
runners.comment: Reappearance this term was encouraging; looks a serious each-way contender
runners.owner: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks

runners.name: Klassleader
runners.spotlight: Made his debut only last June and has had just four races; long odds-on for 1m4f novice win in August and best form when second of six to Sing Us A Song on handicap debut at Haydock (1m6f, again good to soft; came from the back) in September; probably still a work in progress at that stage and he starts 2026 (for in-form yard) with significant potential.
runners.comment: Only four starts; best form when 2nd on handicap debut in September; potential still there

runners.name: Plage De Havre
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year off 11lb lower; competitive almost every time last season, ending on a major high with 4l win at Haydock (1m4f, good) in July; however, there have been a couple of blips and last month's poor show in Listed race at Kempton was one of those; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Third last year off 11lb lower; poor show at Kempton last month and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Sing Us A Song
runners.spotlight: Disappointed over 1m4f at Royal Ascot and Glorious Goodwood last summer but returned from a gelding operation to win at Haydock (sole run at 1m6f, good to soft) in September; made a satisfactory reappearance at Epsom 15 days ago and is still lightly raced, so could have more to offer; good ground and softer would probably be better for him than good to firm.
runners.comment: Made a satisfactory reappearance at Epsom 15 days ago; could have more to offer

runners.name: Dark Moon Rising
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023; no joy anywhere last season (mostly at this track) but the closest he came was a neck defeat here (1m2f; 4lb higher today) on final start; not far away on AW last three starts this year and the same may be possible on today's return to turf.
runners.comment: Not far away on AW last three starts this year; the same may be possible on turf return

runners.name: Stressfree
runners.spotlight: Beaten a head in this race last year and fourth of 22 here in the Ebor in August; began 2026 with another solid effort, when second at Ripon; not a prolific winner with his waiting tactics but big each-way chance.
runners.comment: Beaten a head in this race last year; not a prolific winner but big each-way chance

runners.name: Prince Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Two good runs last August included a narrow win at Goodwood (1m4f, good to firm) but he was soundly beaten on starts either side of those; sold out of Ralph Beckett's stable for 30,000gns in October and not disgraced on reappearance at Ripon 18 days ago; tongue tied last four starts and now also tries cheekpieces; others are a bit more persuasive.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on reappearance at Ripon 18 days ago; others are a bit more persuasive

runners.name: King's Code
runners.spotlight: Second at this meeting in 2024 but only eighth in this race last year; had an excellent autumn on AW and won again in January, but he's struggled to make an impact in recent AW starts and his record is a lot stronger at up to 1m3f than at about 1m4f.
runners.comment: Struggled to make impact in recent AW starts; record much stronger at up to 1m3f than 1m4f

runners.name: Will Scarlet
runners.spotlight: Four French wins for Andre Fabre and thereabouts for new trainers in four consecutive races in Dubai this January-March, winning by 4l from the front in a 1m4f handicap on final occasion; first equipped with cheekpieces that day (they are retained) and it was a tactical switch too; could have more to give with that combination but his handicap mark has been raised significantly.
runners.comment: Ex-French; won latest Meydan handicap (1m4f) for new yard by 4l, from front in cheekpieces

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Four wins (22 races) at up to 1m2f on AW/good to soft when trained in France, before 100,000euros sale last July; finishing towards rear when an outsider in warm races at Kempton (1m3f, AW; hooded) and Musselburgh (1m1f; usual tongue-tie back on) was not a great start to his British career but he's been dropped 3lb after both of those races.
runners.comment: Four wins in France but not a great start to British career; dropped 3lb both British runs
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Paddy The Squire
runners.spotlight: Short-headed on 2024 reappearance and won it last year; thrived last season when winning three races and not far away in the other three, including at this track; highly respected for that campaign, and first-time-out record, but the handicapper reacted and he now needs to resume improvement.
runners.comment: Handicapper reacted to last season's excellent campaign; needs to resume improvement

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who, having been gelded, won two in a row over 6f on AW earlier this year; further progress cannot be ruled out but he's finished seventh on his last two starts and needs something extra today.
runners.comment: Kicked off 2026 with 2 wins in a row but seventh on last two starts; needs something extra

runners.name: Fahrenheit Seven
runners.spotlight: Two wins on turf last season for Mark Usher (5f/6f) and he's continued to improve for his new yard, winning two in a row on AW earlier this year (5f); not at his best when seventh of 15 back on grass in a recent Listed race at Naas (5f, good) but shaped as though this return to 6f could be a positive; returns to a handicap and remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW this year; beaten back on turf latest but still one to be interested in

runners.name: American Style
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ripon (6f, good) on last year's reappearance and ran well in defeat on next four starts (including over C&D) prior to two lesser runs to finish his campaign; returned to form with very close third of 16 in same Ripon race last month and this front-runner is an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Very close 3rd of 16 at Ripon on reappearance & this front-runner is an each-way contender

runners.name: Binhareer
runners.spotlight: Promising 3yo last season and things clicked in a major way with a dominant win in a big-field C&D handicap (good) last October when last seen; he's entered in the Group 1 Jubilee Stakes at Royal Ascot and this lightly raced sort is in excellent hands to continue to progress; strong contender.
runners.comment: Dominant in big-field C&D h'cap last October & in excellent hands to continue to progress

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: 6f German Listed win for Archie Watson last June and looked as good as ever when 1l third of 13 to Strike Red in recent Leicester handicap (good to firm), when back up to 6f on his third run for this yard; may not be far away.
runners.comment: Close third of 13 at Leicester last month when back up to 6f and he may not be far away
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Pilgrim
runners.spotlight: Mixed fortunes since winning the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot in 2024 (good to firm); got back on track to win at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month despite being denied a clear run; his peak performances have come at 5f but he was a C&D winner in his 2yo season; respected up just 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Got back on track to win at Musselburgh last month and he's respected up just 3lb

runners.name: Wicket Keeper
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won maiden/novice races on turf last May/June and was a promising fourth of 14 at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March on his stable debut; however, not quite at the same level there (6f) since and he's up in grade this afternoon; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; possesses some likeable form but he's up in grade & others appeal more

runners.name: Tuco Salamanca
runners.spotlight: Five wins last year (AW/soft) and he improved again to win in good style on his reappearance at Kempton (6f, AW) in March; he's 5-9 on AW as opposed to 1-7 on turf but there can be optimism that he is just as good on grass (has shown promise on various ground) and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Improved again to win in good style at Kempton in March and he's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Pocklington
runners.spotlight: Produced an uncharacteristic below-par run at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month but he's had wind surgery since and was progressive on AW in the winter; twice finished close-up in big-field handicaps over this C&D (good/good to firm) last summer and there's every chance he will be bang there.
runners.comment: Wind op after below-par latest run; progressive previously and has strong C&D form

runners.name: We Never Stop
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf winner who ran well when third at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March; however, the AW seems preferable nowadays and he finished last at Newmarket (5f, good) last month when back on grass.
runners.comment: Ran well on Newcastle AW two starts ago but he seems inferior on turf nowadays

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Merely fifth on his last two starts, following a very promising second on his reappearance at Newcastle (6f, AW); however, he won this last year (good to firm) and is handicapped to be in the mix if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Merely 5th on last two runs but won this last year and is on a competitive mark if on song

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: Three handicap wins during last season's 3yo campaign (6f/5f, including on good/good to firm) and the return of cheekpieces may be a plus; however, he's been unplaced on both runs this year; has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Three wins during last season's 3yo campaign but unplaced on both runs this year

runners.name: Sondad
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last May/June (6f, soft/good to firm) when this visor first went on and he ran well to be placed on his final two outings last autumn; each-way claims if fully tuned up for this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Did well last season after this visor was applied; each-way claims if fully tuned up

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 19 starts going back to June 2023 but, following a wind op, he was an encouraging fifth off his reduced mark behind Strike Red on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Leicester (6f, good to firm), where he was short of room late on; could build on that with a big run today.
runners.comment: Encouraging 5th on recent stable debut at Leicester, where short of room; possible player

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with close third at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month then snapped a losing run when winning at Leicester (6f, good to firm); there's still mileage in his mark judged on old form (including two C&D wins); chance.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester latest; this 2-time C&D winner still has mileage in his mark on old form

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far at Newmarket (6f, good) and Newbury (6f, good to firm) last May and he's well handicapped on that form; however, below best on final two starts last season and likewise on recent reappearance at Newmarket.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but there was no return to form on his recent reappearance at Newmarket

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: Two US Listed wins in 2024 and he twice went close on the St Moritz ice this February; having been detached he kept on for sixth of 11 on his recent stable debut at Goodwood (5f, good); may not be a forlorn hope back up to 6f.
runners.comment: Detached before keeping on over 5f at Goodwood; might not be a forlorn hope back up to 6f

runners.name: Sir Yoshi
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat in two 5f Listed races here when trained by David Marnane but well beaten on stable debut over this C&D (good) last October, and a career-best is needed in first-time cheekpieces on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Has some good course form but a career-best is needed on this comeback; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Desert Falcon
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who performed with great credit on all five starts last season, finishing second at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) on his reappearance and beaten just a neck in the Ayr Gold Cup (6f, soft) when last seen; has had wind surgery since; no surprise if he makes a bold bid on this return.
runners.comment: Consistent front-runner who went close in Ayr Gold Cup when last seen; has gone well fresh

runners.name: Tropical Storm
runners.spotlight: His best form is at 5f and he hasn't been at the top of his game since a Listed win at this meeting last May; however, he kept on well for third at Goodwood (5f, good) recently, suggesting the return to 6f is worth exploring, and his two career wins have both come here; a visor goes on.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Goodwood last time and both career wins have come here

runners.name: Woven
runners.spotlight: 10yo who may prefer slow ground but the going was good when he was fourth of 21 in this in 2024; often runs well here and he reappeared with a creditable fourth of 19 at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) in March; each-way possible.
runners.comment: 10yo; 4th in this in 2024; creditable 4th at Doncaster on reappearance; each-way possible
runners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten this spring on first two starts for new yard; however, he's 1lb lower than when second of 16 in this race last year for Richard Hannon, and he won here in 2023; capable of bouncing back with a bold show.
runners.comment: Yet to shine for new yard but he's 1lb lower than when second of 16 in this last year

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who held on by a neck in last year's Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) and had excuses for her subsequent defeats in 2025; not disgraced, despite failing to justify favouritism, in Newmarket Group 3 on reappearance; again respected at the weights.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly who won last year's Commonwealth Cup; respected at the weights
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Elmonjed
runners.spotlight: Steps up further in class but he has progressive York form and brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D (gained last win cosily in good-ground Listed race) and 4-9 in headgear, which is reapplied with Newmarket reappearance under his belt; has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D and 4-9 in headgear; one to consider

runners.name: American Affair
runners.spotlight: Has built up a good strike rate over 5f/5.5f and registered a neck success at Royal Ascot (good to firm) upped to Group 1 level when last seen; landed a handicap at this meeting 12 months ago; very progressive and has possibilities provided he's as effective back at 6f.
runners.comment: Very progressive, particularly at 5f/5.5f; Group 1 success at Royal Ascot when last seen

runners.name: Cool Hoof Luke
runners.spotlight: Missed last season; retains every ounce of ability judged on AW performances this year, last time landing a Listed prize at Wolverhampton (7f) to take his overall record to 3-6; possibly still has further improvement in him; interesting returned to the C&D of his 2024 Gimcrack win.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 features wins in the Gimcrack over C&D in 2024 and AW Listed race last time

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive over 6f, mostly in handicaps prior to showing bigger things this season by bagging Listed honours at Doncaster (good to soft) and finishing a good second in Group 3 at Newmarket (good); at the top of his form and may rate higher still; in the mix.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; good second in Newmarket Group 3 most recently; in the mix

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has a strike-rate of 12-34 overall and 3-4 at York, the most recent course success in 7f Group 3 last June (equally effective at 6f); ties in with some of today's opponents on penultimate effort; removal of usual hood backfired last time; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-4 at York, the most recent success in Group 3 last June

runners.name: Regional
runners.spotlight: Still capable of classy form, having been placed in the last two runnings of the 5f Group 1 at Royal Ascot; however, he's on a losing run, being 0-8 since landing the Sprint Cup at Haydock in 2023; getting on and returns to action as an 8yo.
runners.comment: Still capable of classy form but he's 0-8 since landing the 2023 Sprint Cup

runners.name: Big Mojo
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good ground or firmer; drops back in grade for this seasonal debut and even under a 5lb penalty holds a major chance on best Group 1 form last term, namely close second in the July Cup and success in the Sprint Cup (beat Kind Of Blue by just over 1l; both well drawn).
runners.comment: Second in the July Cup and won the Sprint Cup; major contender even with a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Kind Of Blue
runners.spotlight: Very smart performer whose Group 1 record features a win on Champions Day at Ascot (6f, soft) in 2024 and second-place finishes in the last two runnings of the Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good); leading player at the weights, assuming he's in top form (disappointing back at Ascot when last seen).
runners.comment: Group 1 form features a win at Ascot in 2024 and two second-place finishes in Sprint Cup

runners.name: Crestofdistinction
runners.spotlight: Produced peak performance over C&D, namely close second to Elmonjed in Listed event; however, faces a stiffer assignment in this higher grade and his reappearance effort (heavy defeat in Newmarket Group 3) hardly bodes well.
runners.comment: Produced peak performance over C&D but faces a stiffer task in this field

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Listed sprint double at Lingfield this year; has lots of good form on turf, including very close third in this race two years ago and respectable fourth in Newmarket Group 3 last time; still a maiden (0-19) in this sphere, however.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate on AW; has lots of good turf form but is 0-19 in this sphere

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Ran well in last year's Nunthorpe in sole York attempt, finishing 100-1 second, but that's very much an outlier in his form; still needs to prove he can repeat it; failed to beat a rival in Newmarket Group 3 contest on reappearance.
runners.comment: Still needs to repeat the form of his 100-1 second in the 2025 Nunthorpe

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced in last three appearances (various grades) but finished behind some of today's rivals in those contests and needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing sequence that goes back to Group 3 honours two years ago.
runners.comment: Needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing run that goes back two years

runners.name: Moments Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Worth a second look given she's the sole Ballydoyle runner and it's possible she'll improve for the step up in trip on her second start of the season; however, she's been beaten in four Listed/Group races since winning a maiden at Leopardstown on her debut last June, and was 2l behind Legacy Link when fifth in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket last October.
runners.comment: Sole Ballydoyle runner so worth a second look but 0-4 since debut win & improvement needed
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sea The Storm
runners.spotlight: Trained as 2yo by David Menuisier (same owners); won on debut at Sandown (1m, soft) last September and runner-up in German Group 3 (1m, soft) in October; narrowly defied penalty in a novice at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) on her recent stable/seasonal debut; she's unexposed and may well have more to offer but has to take a big step forward from that reappearance win.
runners.comment: Runner-up in German Group 3, with maiden/novice wins either side; needs a step forward

runners.name: Legacy Link
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Haydock (1m, good) last September on her second start and rallied for fourth of ten in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (good) in October, the form of which has worked out very well; her dam (a sister to Frankel) was a 1m4f winner and this step up in trip should be right up her street; leading claims for top yard that has won this seven times since 2011.
runners.comment: Fourth in Group 1 Fillies' Mile; form has worked out well and the step up in trip can suit

runners.name: Felicitas
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and again had something to spare when making it 2-2 in a novice at Sandown (1m2f, good) last month; she goes up in grade today but this exciting prospect appeals as a type who will keep on getting better and better; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 2-2, winning those novices with something to spare; up in grade but an exciting prospect

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Her four juvenile starts yielded one win (1m AW maiden on third outing) and she was a clear second of four in a Class 4 handicap at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) on last month's reappearance; she can win more races but dramatic improvement is necessary if she's to take this.
runners.comment: Clear second in AW handicap on reappearance but dramatic improvement is necessary today

runners.name: K Sarra
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for comfortable win at Southwell (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start, then finished down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) last month on her reappearance; however, the drop back in trip looked very much against her and this sister to Pride Of Arras, who won last season's Dante over C&D for connections, could be an entirely different proposition over today's longer trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Down the field in Fred Darling; could be entirely different proposition at the longer trip

runners.name: Inishbeg
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in Thirsk maiden (7f, good) last time, opening his account at the fourth attempt; may well build on that success and show further improvement, in keeping with his pedigree (siblings include a very useful performer for his connections); respected.
runners.comment: Prevailed in Thirsk maiden last time; may well build on that success and progress further
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo season with a hat-trick; consistent efforts this term, good third at Beverley (7.5f) most recently; getting no respite from the assessor but is reasonably solid and likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Ended his 2yo season with a hat-trick; consistent efforts this term; solid chance
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Startled
runners.spotlight: Got up in the final strides upped to 7f at the Newmarket Craven meeting (good) last time, taking record in turf handicaps to 2-2; could well improve further and he represents last year's winning yard; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Newmarket win (upped to 7f) took his record in turf handicaps to 2-2; shortlisted

runners.name: Cotai Lights
runners.spotlight: 6f AW scorer who has made the frame over 7f in both turf attempts, including at Newmarket last time when fitted with cheekpieces (retained); however, likely needs some improvement to double his tally.
runners.comment: 6f AW scorer who has made the frame over 7f in both turf attempts, including last time

runners.name: Al Najashi
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-5, all over 6f/7f on AW this year, since handicapping; registered the latest success on finals day at Newcastle; major contender, assuming his tremendous progress can be sustained back on turf.
runners.comment: Major contender, provided his tremendous AW progress can be sustained back on turf

runners.name: Hasbro Market
runners.spotlight: Doesn't seem totally straightforward but he has a solid record (2122) and may do better still; gelded prior to good effort behind Passing Thought at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on handicap/seasonal debut; in the mix.
runners.comment: Has a solid record and may do better still; good second to Passing Thought on reappearance

runners.name: Montague Menace
runners.spotlight: Possibly didn't take to Epsom (8.5f) last time when beaten favourite on handicap debut; recorded a ready success in Lingfield maiden (7f, AW) in his only other start this term and may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Possibly didn't take to Epsom last time on handicap debut; may still have more to offer

runners.name: Luansobe
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who most recently belied his 40-1 odds with success at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on seasonal debut; needs to prove he can back up that form and also needs to step up sharply on his sole turf effort.
runners.comment: Belied his 40-1 odds with AW reappearance win; needs to prove he can back up that form

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante who seems to face a difficult task and has something to prove off a mark that stems from a 7lb rise for her apparent good effort in the Fred Darling; failed to back up that form last time.
runners.comment: Failed to back up her Fred Darling effort last time; something to prove off her mark

runners.name: Advance Twentyfive
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in valuable contest over C&D last autumn but remains a maiden and has a bit to prove off his mark in this warm handicap; failed to show his form at Thirsk (behind Inishbeg) on reappearance.
runners.comment: Below form at Thirsk (behind Inishbeg) on reappearance and remains a maiden

runners.name: Awraad
runners.spotlight: Ran well over 6f in nursery at the York Ebor festival and Listed event at Ripon last August; nowhere near that form in two outings since and has enough to prove off current mark back at 7f.
runners.comment: Good efforts over 6f last August; nowhere near that form in two outings since

runners.name: Gold Queen Kindly
runners.spotlight: Became a tad exposed for Karl Burke and finished behind a couple of today's rivals, admittedly not disgraced, at Wetherby (7f) most recently; subsequently sold for 28,000gns; 0-7 since debut win.
runners.comment: Became a tad exposed for Karl Burke; 0-7 since debut win; first start for new yard

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well in valuable event over C&D last autumn but needs to prove he can transfer that form to handicap level; failed to do so at Pontefract on reappearance, albeit perhaps needed the run.
runners.comment: Needs to prove the form of his autumn C&D effort can be transferred to handicap level

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in 6f novice event at this meeting last year; has become quite exposed kept to sprinting since but this combination of new trip and return to the Dante festival may have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Combination of new trip and return to the Dante festival may have positive effect

runners.name: First Time
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign for Harry Charlton by winning a Newmarket maiden (6f) and running well in Newcastle handicap (7f, AW); sold for 90,000gns and gelded since; likely needs further progress on stable debut.
runners.comment: Ran well in final 2yo start for Harry Charlton but likely needs further progress

races.race_name: Stuey Weston & Friends Getting Out Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Six wins last July-October, with the initial success over 1m2f/1m1f followed by four wins at 1m or 7f; good second on 1m1f reappearance this term but a disappointing favourite two weeks later; even if he's back to form, this sort of trip (tried twice before) poses a big question.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite last time; this sort of trip (tried twice) poses a big question
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Great Bedwyn
runners.spotlight: Won this (good to firm) on return last year by a neck off 4lb higher than today; subsequent efforts have never quite lived up to that, albeit runner-up on Kempton AW last September and this March; first 1m2f run for ages when disappointing on latest start; could bounce back with each-way claims.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 4lb higher; could bounce back (from latest start) with e-w claims

runners.name: Fox Journey
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024 when trained by Sir Michael Stoute (below form last year for Kevin Ryan) but second of 14 off almost a career-low mark at Musselburgh (1m4f, good; under Zak Wheatley) one month ago was easily his most competitive show since; back up 2lb and needs to build on that revival.
runners.comment: No win since May 2024 but Musselburgh 2nd last time was his most competitive show since

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Had four wins last year (three 1m, one 1m2f) and has run with credit on all four starts this term, but his only attempt over this far was in 2024; needs a career best to get his head in front again.
runners.comment: Ran with credit all four starts 2026; needs a career best to win again; stamina to prove

runners.name: Military Cross
runners.spotlight: Second or third every time in September-January in his seven races for the Gosdens, most AW; tried as far as 1m6f and in two forms of headgear; sold for 65,000gns in February and has now had wind surgery and has his tongue tied first time; competitive mark in theory but clearly questions to answer.
runners.comment: Has competitive mark in theory, but clearly questions to answer; left Gosdens in February

runners.name: Mafting
runners.spotlight: Failed to crack it in 2025 handicaps but reappeared to win off a reduced mark in nine-runner race at Redcar (1m2f) this April; second of six when 10-11 at Doncaster (1m4f, again good to firm) 12 days later, which was just as good on form; each-way contender but a bit extra is needed if he's to take this.
runners.comment: Failed to crack it in 2025 handicaps; 1st and 2nd this year in Class 5, but e-w contender

runners.name: Project Geofin
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer, with two minor honours in the autumn including one here; ended last year on a low note and started this one with another, while pedigree leaves him with plenty to prove on this first go at 1m4f.
runners.comment: Ended last year on a low note and same on return; plenty to prove on first go at 1m4f

runners.name: Arrange
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up over 2m from five visits here, plus fourth of 20 in this race in 2024; ran creditably over 1m4f on 2025 return and is 11lb lower for this reappearance, but others seem to be banging on the door more loudly.
runners.comment: Twice 2nd over 2m from five visits here; others seem to be banging on the door more loudly
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

runners.name: Master Builder
runners.spotlight: Last seen floundering on heavy going in the November Handicap at Doncaster; the clear highpoint of that campaign was his second at Epsom (1m4f, good; stays 1m6f) in April, which came first time out and points up his chance given that he is now 11lb lower; needs a second look.
runners.comment: Clear highpoint of last campaign was second at Epsom in April; needs a second look

runners.name: Minhad
runners.spotlight: Ran poorly on final start last term but gelded soon afterwards and, with visor/blinkers dispensed with, he returned with a narrow win in 12-runner race at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last month, gradually working his way to the front in final strides under 7lb claimer Kyle McHugh; 4lb higher today but could have more to offer in what's only his second campaign.
runners.comment: Narrow 1m4f win at Thirsk last month; 4lb higher but could have more to offer

runners.name: Galyx
runners.spotlight: Peak efforts have been on soft or heavy ground, including two wins as 3yo and a creditable place last June when last seen (1m3f; not proven over this far); only eight races in total and he could still have more to give, but substantial rain may be needed; withdrawn twice recently when it didn't arrive.
runners.comment: Peak efforts have been on soft or heavy ground; substantial rain may be needed

runners.name: Hermetic
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground winner (1m4f/2m) in France for Christophe Ferland last autumn, from just four races in total; picked up for 105,000euros in November; no surprise were he to develop into another useful handicapper for this yard and he needs a check for any confidence behind him in the betting market; withdrawn when the ground was good to firm at Ascot on Saturday.
runners.comment: Dual soft-ground winner in France last autumn, from just four races; needs a market check

runners.name: Lightening Company
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 5075 at this meeting in the last four years; one win a season had previously appeared his quota but he's already exceeded that in 2026, having scored at Pontefract (1m4f/1m2f) on last two outings; that demands some respect but this race is a higher grade.
runners.comment: Form figures 5075 at this meeting last four years; won latest two starts in lower grade
runners.owner: Middleham Park Cxv, Lycett Rcg & Partner

runners.name: Helmsley
runners.spotlight: Left Ralph Beckett after 1m2f Redcar novice win (good to soft) in October; that form has not been reached in her five handicaps since, most at 1m but never dangerous latest when back up to 1m4f at Thirsk; others preferred.
runners.comment: Left R Beckett after 1m2f novice win in October; not so good in her five handicaps since

runners.name: Free World
runners.spotlight: First foal; half-brother to recent 1m2f debut winner Fighter's Spirit; dam French 7f winner (RPR 80), half-sister to three Listed winners headed by Elite Army (113); had three runs at Meydan in winter 2024-25 for Osama Refai, beaten about 9l when ninth of 15 in a 1m4f handicap on final occasion; flimsy claims on this first run since.
runners.comment: Had three runs at Meydan in winter 2024-25 for Osama Refai; flimsy claims on this return

runners.name: Marhaba The Champ
runners.spotlight: Formerly useful for Kevin Ryan but was sold last July and has been comfortably held since on the Flat and over hurdles; this tongue-tie (tried once in 2023) returned on latest outing, along with first-time hood; 17lb below his peak mark, but no win since he scored twice at York in 2023.
runners.comment: Comfortably held since sold last July; 17lb below peak mark but no win since 2023

runners.name: Sportingsilvermine
runners.spotlight: Did well last spring and placed five consecutive times last summer/autumn; now well handicapped on those exploits but down the field in his three appearances this year; latest was in a 1m4f race at Chester last week, where he'd been runner-up the year before, so there are doubts currently.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on exploits last term but down the field in his three runs this year

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Tonal
runners.spotlight: Has won five times on the AW since October, most recently at Chelmsford (1m, AW; second has won since) in March, and another solid effort at Southwell last month; 0-9 on grass but he's on a much lower mark on this surface.
runners.comment: Has won five times on the AW since October; 0-9 on turf but his mark reflects that

runners.name: Gladiadora
runners.spotlight: Winning return from short break at Southwell (7f, AW) in March and went close at Nottingham (8.5f, good) on her next start; came home nicely when fourth behind three more prominently ridden rivals at Lingfield (7f, AW) 16 days ago and should appreciate returning to this trip; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Went close at Nottingham last month and came home nicely over 7f last time; back up to 1m
runners.owner: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining

runners.name: Queen Of Good News
runners.spotlight: Had undergone wind surgery prior to C&D win on reappearance last month but never featured at Doncaster since; could be suited by the return to this track (form figures here read 221) and she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month; never featured at Doncaster but could be happier back here

runners.name: Cuban Girl
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Brighton (1m, good to firm) last June when trained by Andrew Balding but she's struggled in three starts since joining this stable; switches back to turf having been dropped another 3lb.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at Brighton for Andrew Balding but struggled in three starts for this stable

runners.name: Campani
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when winning at Brighton (6f, good; first-time hood) in August; hood removed when coming home nicely to make a winning return from a three-month break at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in January and has been off again since; leaves the impression the step back up to 1m will prove no issue.
runners.comment: Came home nicely over 7f at Chelmsford in January; off since and now steps back up to 1m

runners.name: Suitcase Smith
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely here (1m3.5f) on his handicap debut in October and it was the same story on his return at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) 36 days ago; may settle better returned to this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Too free over further in two starts in handicaps; may settle better over this shorter trip

runners.name: Hello Cotai
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner for Adam West, two of them at Epsom (8.5f) in 2024 and the other at Chantilly over 9.5f last April; he ran well at Lingfield (1m2f) on his stable debut 15 days ago and should come on for that reappearance; holds solid claims returned to this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Three-time winner for Adam West; ran well over 1m2f on his stable debut; solid claims

runners.name: El Fox
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but she ran respectably in handicaps last year, including when fourth at Wolverhampton in October; each-way claims returned to turf on her first start since.
runners.comment: 0-8 but she posted some respectable efforts in handicaps last year; off since October

runners.name: Kamaway
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify joint-favouritism when behind Gladiadora at Southwell (7f, AW) on her handicap debut in March but that run can be easily excused as she was denied a clear passage; steps back up to 1m and could be capable of better.
runners.comment: Denied a clear passage on her handicap debut at Southwell in March; capable of better

runners.name: Siam Fox
runners.spotlight: C&D winner but his last success came back in June 2024; failed to beat a rival home at Doncaster on his first start since undergoing wind surgery and needs to take a big step forward from that.
runners.comment: Without a win since June 2024 and failed to beat a rival home at Doncaster on his return

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m winner on turf (good) and AW; shaped quite well on the AW on his first two starts this year but blew his chance at the start when down the field at Thirsk next time and could only finish midfield at Nottingham (1m2f, good) 25 days ago; drops in grade.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner; only midfield at Nottingham (1m2f) latest but now drops down in grade

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over 5f at Wolverhampton since joining this yard, with the most recent success coming off 3lb lower; solid effort at Southwell (6f) 13 days ago but now steps up in grade on this switch back to turf.
runners.comment: Has won twice over 5f at Wolverhampton since joining this yard; now steps up in grade

runners.name: Kinswoman
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; progressive last year, winning on three occasions, including when making all at Haydock (5f, good) when last seen in October; now 5lb higher but this well-bred filly looks the type to continue progressing as a 4yo; holds an entry in Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot.
runners.comment: Progressive as 3yo; 5lb higher than for Haydock win; holds Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot

runners.name: Michaela's Boy
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; won at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January on second start since returning to this stable and he went close over that same C&D next time; ran creditably back on turf at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago but he has a better strike-rate on the AW (1-24 on turf).
runners.comment: Ran creditably at Nottingham last time but his strike-rate on turf now reads 1-24

runners.name: Almaty Star
runners.spotlight: Had been running well on the AW this winter and wasn't for catching after setting a strong pace over this C&D last month (third has won since); an 8lb rise still leaves him on a fair mark based on his previous form but he does now step back up in grade.
runners.comment: Wasn't for catching after setting a strong pace over C&D last time; up 8lb and in grade

runners.name: Naana's Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won at Redcar (5f, good to firm) and Goodwood (5f, heavy) last summer, but he was around 5l behind Kinswoman at Haydock (5f, good; first-time tongue-tie) in October on final start for Alice Haynes; watch the betting on debut for new trainer.
runners.comment: Won twice last year but was behind Kinswoman at Haydock in October; new trainer

runners.name: Grandlad
runners.spotlight: Can front-run, as when making all on his reappearance at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago; a 3lb rise for that success looks fair but he's likely to face competition for the lead here.
runners.comment: Made all at Nottingham on his reappearance; likely to face competition for the lead

runners.name: Twilight Fun
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose last four wins have been on the AW, the most recent over 6f at Newcastle in February, but he did win here (6f) in 2024; failed to beat a rival home having unseated his rider on the way to the start at Newcastle in March and now drops back to 5f on first start since; looks likely to face competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Last four wins have been on the AW; failed to beat a rival home at Newcastle in March

runners.name: Existent
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run at Southwell in January and posted a respectable effort at Newmarket (5f, good) last month; however, he was down the field at Ascot (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago and is now just 1-46 on turf; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ended a long losing run at Southwell in January; well beaten at Ascot and now 1-46 on turf

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Four wins from 40 starts, with the two wins on turf coming at Windsor (good to firm) in 2025; returned from a 65-day break with a good run at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago, beaten a head by Grandlad; contender.
runners.comment: Both turf wins at Windsor but narrowly beaten by Grandlad at Nottingham last time

runners.name: Cressida Wildes
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last July but she's been well beaten in two starts since landing a handicap at Wolverhampton in February, most recently at Ascot four days ago; bit to prove.
runners.comment: C&D winner but been well beaten in two starts since winning at Wolverhampton in February

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: All three wins over 6f on the AW but he does act on turf; had a wide trip on his return from a break but stepped up on that when fourth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; each-way chance.
runners.comment: All three wins have been on the AW over 6f but was a solid fourth at Ascot (5f) recently

runners.name: Forager
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; progressive last year, winning three times, including over this C&D last May before following up at York (5f, good to firm); below best at Lingfield in November though and she's unlikely to get things her own way in front on her first start since.
runners.comment: Progressed last year; below best in November; unlikely to get things her own way in front

runners.name: Rocking Ends
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on good to firm last year, including over C&D in August off a mark 1lb higher than today's; 0-4 in Bahrain this winter but now returns to Britain on a handy mark having been off for 83 days; could have this run to suit and holds claims.
runners.comment: 0-4 in Bahrain this winter but now 1lb lower than for last year's C&D success; has claims

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Diplomata
runners.spotlight: February foal; 57,000gns foal, 140,000euros yearling; by Havana Grey; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Power Shot (German 8.5f-1m3f) and Aston Clinton (French 7f); dam sprint maiden (RPR 64), half-sister to useful 7f-8.4f winner King's Pavilion and to dam of high-class sprinter King's Apostle; she's interesting for leading trainer having first 2yo runner of the year.
runners.comment: 140,000euros yearling by Havana Grey; yard's first 2yo runner of the year; interesting

runners.name: Dubai Charm
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f winner (including AW; RPR 90), half-sister to winners Brussels (Group-placed 6f 2yo), Love Affairs (6f 2yo) and Lord Lovelace (7f); shaped with promise on her debut at Nottingham (6f, good; 25-1) on her debut 17 days ago and has an experience edge on her rivals.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on her debut at Nottingham; has an experience edge on her rivals

runners.name: Scommessa Sicura
runners.spotlight: February foal; 88,000euros foal, 100,000gns yearling; by Cracksman; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Stanhope Gardens (Group-placed 7.4f 2yo/1m; RPR 113), Inspiritus (9.4f/1m2f AW; 97) and Corky (1m2f/12.5f; 88); dam Listed-placed 7f/1m2f winner (101), half-sister to St Leger runner-up Romsdal; nice pedigree and needs a market check but may be one for longer trips.
runners.comment: 88,000euros foal, 100,000gns yearling; dam Listed-placed 7f/1m2f winner; may want further

runners.name: Charcoal Diva
runners.spotlight: March foal; 3,000gns yearling; by Bated Breath; second foal; half-sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Marwaan (RPR 68); dam placed 6f (including 2yo/AW; 67), half-sister to winners Montatham (smart 7f/1m) and Troubador (useful 6f 2yo), out of 6f-1m2f winner; best watched on debut unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: March foal; half-sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Marwaan (RPR 68); best watched on her debut
runners.owner: B Keane, S Nugent & G4 Contracts

runners.name: Deja Vu Diva
runners.spotlight: February foal; by Dream Ahead; 2,500gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Rockprincess (6f 2yo; RPR 64) and Leontynka (Czech 6.5f/7f); unraced dam closely related to 7f/7.4f winner Kodiac Pride, out of 6f winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Zoffany; stable 0-16 with 2yos.
runners.comment: February foal; 2,500gns yearling; half-sister to two winners; yard 0-16 with 2yos

runners.name: Jazzy Blue
runners.spotlight: March foal; by Showcasing; 20,000euros yearling; sixth foal; sister to 1m AW winner Persian Jewel (RPR 87), half-sister to Japanese 6f/7f winner Blue Spirit (including 2yo; 107); dam 1m2f winner (94), out of half-sister to 7f Listed winner Secret Garden (dam of Irish 2,000 winner Roderic O'Connor); watch the betting on debut for yard that's 3-9 with 2yos this year.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; sister to 1m AW winner Persian Jewel; yard 3-9 with 2yos this year

runners.name: Rocsane
runners.spotlight: March foal; 16,000gns yearling; by Persian Force; third foal; dam well held 6f/7f 2yo in France, closely related to Group-placed 1m winner Cawdor, out of Group-placed 1m2f winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Headman; likely best watched on her debut.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; dam well held 6f/7f 2yo in France; best watched on her debut

runners.name: Silent Beauty
runners.spotlight: January foal; 1,000,000euros yearling; by Night Of Thunder; second foal; dam 7f-1m winner (including 2yo/Group 1; RPR 111), half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Heavenly Being, out of 5f 2yo Listed winning sister to HK 6f Group 1 winner Peniaphobia, half-sister to Group-placed 5f winner Safari Sunset; has an attractive pedigree and this expensive purchase is one to note on her debut.
runners.comment: 1,000,000euros yearling; out of Group 1 winner No Speak Alexander; lots to like on paper

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Butterfly Beach
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite for each of her first three starts but has yet to progress from her debut effort at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last June; off since finishing third of seven in a handicap at Newcastle (5f, AW) in November but should appreciate this step back up in trip; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on the pick of last year's efforts and should be happier back up to 6f

runners.name: Creative Queen
runners.spotlight: 800,000euros Breeze-up 2yo; found one too good when favourite for three-runner novice at Newcastle on debut in September but could only manage midfield on reappearance at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; overhauled inside the final furlong after hanging left over this C&D 15 days ago and the blinkers now go on; a contender again.
runners.comment: Overhauled inside the final furlong after hanging left over C&D; blinkers now go on

runners.name: Reigning Queen
runners.spotlight: Dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 110); showed ability when fourth of eight on her debut at Southwell (5f, AW) 15 days ago and should come on for that experience; steps up to 6f with claims on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability when fourth at Southwell over 5f on her debut; has claims upped to 6f

runners.name: Secret Harvest
runners.spotlight: 4,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Prisoner's Dilemma (Listed-placed 6f/7f including AW; RPR 110) and Epic Express (6f-1m including AW; 83); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (74), half-sister to US 1m Grade 2 winner Mr Napper Tandy; best watched unless the betting speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: 4,000gns yearling; dam 1m AW 2yo winner (RPR 74); market can guide on debut

runners.name: Viviana
runners.spotlight: Out of a sister to 5f/6f Group 3 winner Judicial; showed plenty of promise on her debut at Redcar (6f, good to firm; 22-1) 13 days ago, keeping on for third after getting outpaced earlier; may well take a step forward from that effort.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of promise on her debut, keeping on for third having been outpaced; claims

runners.name: Physique
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner (good to soft/heavy) and he was placed off higher marks than this when trained by Pam Sly last year; below his best in two starts since returning to this yard but he did have excuses at Newmarket 27 days ago having stumbled at the start and sat up with a strong pace; chance now dropped in grade.
runners.comment: Below his best in both starts this year but had excuses last time; not discounted

runners.name: Noble Guest
runners.spotlight: Both of his wins from 16 starts have been over C&D (good/good to soft), the most recent in September off 2lb lower; ran a solid race here 15 days ago on his return from a break and he once again has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Both wins have been over this C&D and he ran a solid race here on his return 15 days ago

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: All three of his wins have been at Kempton (7f/1m) but he was runner-up here over 1m last April (good to firm); has posted two solid efforts on the AW since returning from a break and has place claims back on turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up here over 1m last year; two solid effort since returning from a break; ew claims

runners.name: Shimmering Spin
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; sole win from 16 starts came in a maiden at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in September but he did step forward from his return when fourth in first-time cheekpieces over C&D 22 days ago; headgear is retained.
runners.comment: Stepped forward from his return when fourth in first-time cheekpieces over C&D last time

runners.name: Beagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Went close on his debut last year and got off the mark at the second attempt when winning at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in March on his first start for 329 days; posted a solid effort at Leicester last month when trying to give weight away to some 3yos and he's open to improvement now handicapping.
runners.comment: Solid effort in the face of tough task at Leicester; open to improvement now handicapping

runners.name: Anthropologist
runners.spotlight: Gained his third success when winning at Newcastle (7f, AW) in March having got plenty of cover when there was a headwind; wasn't able to match that effort there 23 days ago and now steps back up in grade; returns to turf with place claims.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in March but wasn't able to match that last time; back up in grade

runners.name: Potomac River
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in each of his three starts last year, most recently when a 1l third of six at Newcastle (7f, AW) in September; has since undergone wind/gelding operations and he's open to further progress now switched to handicap company.
runners.comment: Showed ability last year; has undergone wind/gelding ops since last seen 236 days ago

runners.name: Fifty Nifty
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Tom Clover, including on his debut at this track in April 2024; shaped quite well at Kempton (6f, AW) in December but failed to build on that next time and has since left Michael Appleby; steps back up in trip on his yard debut having undergone wind surgery since last seen; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well at Kempton in November but not so good next time; wind op and yard debut

runners.name: Staniel Cay
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form when dropped back to 5f in his second handicap to beat his 16 rivals at Nottingham (5f, good) 25 days ago, travelling strongly and winning comfortably; up 7lb but this well-related colt looks open to further progress.
runners.comment: Showed improved form when winning over 5f at Nottingham; up 7lb but open to progress

runners.name: London Is Blue
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home at Chelmsford (5f, AW) in October but was progressive on turf prior to that; left the impression his first start for 200 days was needed when fourth at Bath (5f, good) last time and he is of interest with that effort behind him.
runners.comment: Progressive in turf handicaps last year; left the impression that recent outing was needed

runners.name: Grey Horizon
runners.spotlight: Often front-runner and won four times on the AW over 6f this winter; gave it a good go from the front at Lingfield on Good Friday before weakening into sixth, but he was well beaten at Bath (5.5f, good) 26 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won four times on the AW over 6f this winter but was well beaten on turf last time

runners.name: Victor Cee
runners.spotlight: Sole win from nine starts came when making all in a four-runner maiden at Hamilton (6f) last August when trained by Karl Burke; ran respectably at Catterick (5f, good) three weeks ago on his first start since being gelded and he can come on from that first outing since January.
runners.comment: 1-9 but he ran respectably at Catterick last time on his first start since being gelded

runners.name: Koffee And Kale
runners.spotlight: Off the mark by a short head at Leicester (5f novice, good to soft) in October and subsequently gelded; creditable third in handicap at Ripon (6f, good) on return 18 days ago and won't mind the drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Ran a creditable race over 6f at Ripon on his return and won't mind the drop back in trip

runners.name: U S S Charleston
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when winning at Wolverhampton in December and he posted solid efforts on his next three starts; however, he was down the field at Lingfield 40 days ago and now returns to turf.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in December but he was down the field at Lingfield 40 days ago

runners.name: Loleeta
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won a Lingfield AW novice last August; well held in her first two handicaps but ran well under Chloe Lyons when second at Southwell (5f, AW) 18 days ago; needs to back that up on this first turf race since second start.
runners.comment: Ran well under Chloe Lyons when second at Southwell last time; needs to back that up

races.race_name: Murrayshall Country Estate Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kaydence
runners.spotlight: Uttoxeter bumper winner in October (2m1f, good to soft); improved for the step up to 2m5f when runner-up in mares' maiden at Newton Abbot (good; long way clear of the rest) on second hurdle run last month; contender.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who bettered debut run when 2nd in 2m5f mares' maiden last time; chance

runners.name: Frankies Fortune
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner last April (3m, yielding); sound hurdle debut when clear second to an odds-on shot in mares' novice over C&D last month (good; previous winner well back in third); interesting contender.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who was a clear 2nd over C&D on hurdles debut last month; interesting

runners.name: Lowlands
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her four runs over hurdles, last time (December) finishing third in a Kelso mares' handicap (2m2f, good to soft); stays 2m4f and stable going well; chance.
runners.comment: Placed on three of four hurdle starts, in handicaps on last two runs; chance

runners.name: Kayla's Friend
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; fair third in a Huntingdon mares' maiden on hurdle debut in March (2m3f, good); should do better, but looks the stable second string.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; fair third at Huntingdon on hurdles debut; looks the stable second string

runners.name: Oor Wish
runners.spotlight: Has shown some promise but has plenty to find with Frankies Fortune who was 20l in front of her over C&D last month.
runners.comment: Has shown some promise but has plenty to find with Frankies Fortune on C&D form

runners.name: Hunters Spring
runners.spotlight: Newcastle bumper winner in November 2024 (2m1f, good); fair form at up to 2m2f over hurdles; may improve up in trip, but more exposed than most.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who has shown fair form over hurdles; 2m4f should suit; fairly exposed now

runners.name: Bracing Brook
runners.spotlight: Dam a 3m hurdle winner but she's been pulled up on both starts so far, last time over C&D.
runners.comment: Dam a 3m hurdle winner but she's been pulled up on both starts so far, last time over C&D

runners.name: Expensive Emotion
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in November (3m, yielding to soft); creditable fourth over C&D on hurdles debut last month (good to soft; 25-1); should improve on that.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; fourth over C&D when a 25-1 shot on hurdles debut; improvement likely

runners.name: Quinnfinity
runners.spotlight: 150-1 shot when pulled up in a C&D mares' novice on hurdles debut last month; should improve but plenty to find.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a mares' maiden over C&D on hurdles debut last month; plenty to find

runners.name: Poppies In July
runners.spotlight: Winner of a three-runner mares' maiden last month on second run pointing in Ireland (3m, yielding to soft); interesting on hurdles debut having been bought for 60,000euros 11 days later.
runners.comment: 60,000euros buy after winning 3-runner Irish point last month; hurdles/stable debut
runners.owner: Up4b And Molly And Paul Willis

runners.name: Nufinbutdatruth
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off last in two bumpers; needs to improve now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Tailed-off last in two bumpers; needs to improve now going hurdling

races.race_name: Murrayshall Country Estate Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Garde Des Champs
runners.spotlight: Hasn't the best of strike-rates but both his wins have been here, the latest over C&D last July (good) when wearing cheekpieces for the first time; disappointing up in trip here last time (didn't jump well); may have to race run to suit him but will need to jump better.
runners.comment: Not best of strike rates (both wins over C&D); not jump well here last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Hypotenus
runners.spotlight: Won a novice handicap chase at Taunton (2m, good) for Joe Tickle in January and fair third over same C&D next time; changed yards last month having had a wind op, and was a well-beaten third of three at Chepstow on stable debut (2m, good); hung right there so this track should suit better.
runners.comment: Taunton chase win for Joe Tickle; wind op since; could bounce back from poor run last time

runners.name: Next Twist
runners.spotlight: In good form over hurdles when last out, winning a Southwell maiden (2m, good to soft) and an Ayr novice (2m, soft; made all); closely related to a hurdle/chase winner; interesting contender on chase debut; can pull hard.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles earlier in the year; can pull hard; chase debut; interesting

runners.name: Shadowfax Of Rohan
runners.spotlight: Below form over hurdles here last month (2m, good) but had been running well over fences prior to that, with wins at Catterick (1m7f, good to soft; made all) and Southwell (2m, good); up in grade and 5lb out of the handicap, but could go well.
runners.comment: Below par over hurdles here latest; good form over fences before that; 5lb out of handicap
runners.owner: K Jardine R Griffiths W Duffus

runners.name: Dance Thief
runners.spotlight: Won a five-runner handicap chase at Ayr in March (2m, soft) but well beaten both starts since (latest over hurdles); conditions suit; a possible.
runners.comment: Ayr chase win in March but well beaten both starts since; conditions suit; a possible

runners.name: Newtown Rambler
runners.spotlight: Ex-Henry de Bromhead; won a Warwick handicap hurdle on second start for Adrian Keatley in March (2m, good to soft; cheekpieces tried and retained) but pulled up on soft ground at Ayr last time; had four runs over fences in Ireland, best two runs when fourth in a couple of 2m3f handicaps at Wexford in August (good); interesting.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; hurdles winner in headgear for this yard in March; fair chase form; interesting

runners.name: Mister Cessna
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Uttoxeter on chase debut in October (2m, good); decent second next time but last two runs have been a bit disappointing (26l last of four at Stratford last month); may well have the race run to suit him and he's a possible.
runners.comment: Won on chase debut; hasn't progressed but this could be run to suit him and has a chance

runners.name: Carismatic Soldier
runners.spotlight: Only win was in a Chepstow novice on heavy in January 2025 (2m); not at his best last four starts but on a fair mark now; looks stable second string.
runners.comment: Only win was over 2m on heavy; not at his best last 4 starts; looks stable second string

runners.name: Suspected
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles over 3m here in April (good); now 6lb higher, but longer trip should suit and one with a chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark over hurdles over 3m here in April; now 6lb higher; should go well again.

runners.name: Lady In The Park
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap chase over 3m here in April 2025; fair form over fences last season (third over 3m here in April) and could go well on first hurdles run since February 2025; cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Chase winner here; fair form chasing last term; headgear tried; a chance back hurdling

runners.name: Manowest
runners.spotlight: Won a Worcester maiden hurdle last May (2m7f, good); creditable runs handicapping since; conditions suit; chance.
runners.comment: Won 2m7f maiden hurdle last May; fair form in handicaps since; conditions suit; chance

runners.name: Shoeshine Boy
runners.spotlight: Two wins on soft/heavy over 3m at Ayr last January; some fair efforts since; return to this trip should suit but best form on softer ground.
runners.comment: Two Ayr wins on soft/heavy last January; fair form since; better on softer ground

runners.name: Fairlawn Skipper
runners.spotlight: Won a Stratford bumper in July 2025 (2m, good to firm); yet to win over hurdles but several good runs, notably when third in a 2m7f handicap at Newcastle in October; not so good last time but off since; a possible.
runners.comment: Fast-ground bumper winner; several fair runs over hurdles; back from a break; a possible

runners.name: Ebselysees
runners.spotlight: Ex-Noel Williams; 18-1 winner over 3m here in April (good; second dead-heated next time); up 5lb; interesting.
runners.comment: Ex-Noel Williams; 18-1 winner over 3m here last time (form sound); up 5lb; chance

runners.name: Maura Jeanne
runners.spotlight: Thorough stayer whose last win was at Ayr in February 2025 (3m, soft); several good runs since, including when second at Carlisle last month; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 2-13, last win in February 2025; good second at Carlisle last month; each-way chance

runners.name: Buy Some Time
runners.spotlight: Ex-pointer; dual winner over 3m here in 2024 (good); sound run when third over 2m4f here last time; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Ex-pointer; won twice over 3m here in 2024; creditable third over 2m4f here latest; chance

runners.name: Bayonetta
runners.spotlight: Exposed mare; has won one of her 15 races pointing in Ireland (fair third last month) but yet to win in 11 starts over hurdles, although has been placed on six occasions; 3m2f not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Exposed; has won one of her 15 points in Ireland; yet to win over hurdles; 3m2f a concern

runners.name: Dalileo
runners.spotlight: Record of 4-56, latest win coming off this mark over 3m here last July (good) on second run after a break; hasn't been out since November; should come on for the run.
runners.comment: 4-56, latest win over 3m here last July on 2nd run after a break; may come on for the run
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Jirko
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-12) but consistent enough since introduced to fences (32333U); this easing mark is helpful from a handicapping perspective but he needs to brush up his jumping - last two efforts in cheekpieces unconvincing - to threaten the principals.
runners.comment: 0-12; easing mark is helpful from handicapping perspective; needs to brush up his jumping.

runners.name: Sir Carnegie
runners.spotlight: Completed a double with win at Kelso (3m, good to soft; 11-4) last November; wasn't fluent - has been a theme - but bounced back to form in reapplied cheekpieces when chasing home Inis Oirr over C&D (good) three weeks ago; 2lb higher with James Bowen taking over in the saddle; likely candidate.
runners.comment: Back to form when chasing home Inis Oirr over C&D (good) three weeks ago; sits 2lb higher.

runners.name: Burgundy Man
runners.spotlight: 0-20 hurdles but made winning chasing debut over C&D (soft; 7-2, from 2lb lower) last October; well beaten on both subsequent starts, dropping out tamely once headed, finishing well behind Sir Carnegie over C&D three weeks ago; needs to rediscover a spark.
runners.comment: C&D winner (chasing debut) last October; well beaten on both subsequent starts; risky.

runners.name: Similar Story
runners.spotlight: 1-22; has dropped markedly in the weights over the past six months (form figures P5UP03); trainer seems to have his string in better form now, and she did rally from a tough position when modest third at Kelso (3m; good, 22-1) latest; no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: 1-22; rallied from a tough position when modest third at Kelso (3m; good, 22-1) latest.

runners.name: The Flying Poet
runners.spotlight: Runner-up four times over hurdles prior to getting off the mark in a tight finish here (3m, good; 13-8 favourite, added tongue-tie) last September; off since and set to make chasing debut but there's no reason why he shouldn't take to fences; merits respect.
runners.comment: Off the mark in tight finish here (3m, good; 13-8 fav) last September; switches to fences.

runners.name: Aill Dubh
runners.spotlight: Ex Irish; took a while to warm to the task for this trainer but he's getting the hang of things now and there was plenty to like about the manner in which he knuckled down to see off Cahier's Den at Newcastle (3m, good to soft) six weeks ago; still lowly weighted despite a token rise; player.
runners.comment: Knuckled down to see off Cahier's Den at Newcastle (3m, good to soft) 6 weeks ago; player.

runners.name: Attaboyarchie
runners.spotlight: 27-race maiden; last two efforts were respectable, including when runner-up at Down Royal (3m2f, good; beaten 8l) over a month ago; others make more appeal for win purposes.
runners.comment: 27-race maiden; runner-up at Down Royal (3m2f, good; beaten 8l) over a month ago.

runners.name: Defying Gravity
runners.spotlight: Didn't offer much on his first three starts for this trainer, but there has been a slight uptick since wind surgery; third, beaten 22l, over hurdles here (2m, good) latest; hood added for rules chasing debut (0-3 points); task is a tough one from 3lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Third, beaten 22l, over hurdles here (2m, good) latest; hood added for rules chasing debut

races.race_name: 125 Years Of Bruce Farms "Grassroots" Hunters' Chase (Corinthian Spirit Qualifier)

runners.name: Potters Party
runners.spotlight: Ex Henry de Bromhead; unseated rider back under rules at Hexham on penultimate start but bounced back to justify favouritism at Ludlow (3m, good; 7l winner) the following week; top rated and tough to beat if in similar form.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Ludlow (3m, good) recently; top rated and tough to beat.

runners.name: Foxinthebox
runners.spotlight: Won a Huntingdon maiden hurdle for Olly Murphy back in May 2022, but hasn't raced under rules since the summer of 2024; made all, turning over the odds-on favourite, in a three-runner Ladies Open at Pusk (3m, good) 18 days ago; is 2-3 under Amy Milburn.
runners.comment: Made all, turning over odds-on favourite, in three-runner point at Pusk (3m) 18 days ago.

runners.name: Theoldboggroad
runners.spotlight: Won twice in points, the latest in a Mixed Open at Overton (3m, good; 3-1) in March; tailed off in the Buccleuch Cup at Kelso (3m, good; 9-1) recently, so tough to envisage him turning the tables on runner-up Phantoms Cave; cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Tailed off in the Buccleuch Cup at Kelso (3m, good; 9-1); work to do with Phantoms Cave.

runners.name: Deerstalker
runners.spotlight: Last of four (beaten 26l) in this last year; arrives in better form this year, with back-to-back point wins at Overton (3m); however, there wasn't much depth to his latest win, with his main market rival departing at the first fence.
runners.comment: Last of four (btn 26l) last year; race-fit after back-to-back point wins at Overton (3m).

runners.name: Phantoms Cave
runners.spotlight: Won twice in points last season, including by 16l at Overton (2m4f, good; 2-1 favourite) in March; played second fiddle to the talented Boley Bob in the Buccleuch Cup at Kelso (3m, good; 5-1) over three weeks ago; the likeliest threat to Potters Party.
runners.comment: Played second fiddle to Boley Bob in the Buccleuch Cup at Kelso (3m, good; 5-1) recently.

runners.name: Ruler Of The River
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles, including from a mark of 77 at Plumpton (2m4f, good) last September; low-key in points since joining Tom Fife, failing to beat a single rival at Hornby Castle and Witton Castle; most unlikely candidate.
runners.comment: Low-key since joining Tom Fife, failing to beat a rival at Hornby Castle & Witton Castle.

races.race_name: Murrayshall Meetings And Events Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Byron Hill
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdles win came at Carlisle (2m1f, soft; from 8lb higher) in October 2024; consistent this year, without necessarily looking ready to win again; third to better-fancied stablemate Cosmic Soul at Sedgefield (2m4f, good) six weeks ago.
runners.comment: 1-18 hurdles; third to better-fancied stablemate Cosmic Soul at Sedgefield (2m4f) latest.

runners.name: Gonnino
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-12); runner-up three times, including when chasing home Belvedere Bhoy at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft) early last month; took a sharp backward step at Kelso subsequently, so it's tough to have confidence; added cheekpieces may eke out a fraction more.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft) early last month; backward step at Kelso since.

runners.name: Velvet Vulcan
runners.spotlight: Twice narrowly beaten at Ayr (2m4f, soft) in the winter but he has struggled to get competitive at Musselburgh, Ayr and here in his last three starts; mark is manageable and trainer won a division of this last year with Follow Charlie but he needs a revival in fortunes.
runners.comment: Twice narrowly beaten at Ayr (2m4f) in the winter; poor since; trainer won this last year.

runners.name: Getaway Master
runners.spotlight: No reason to recommend on strength of modest efforts this year at Chepstow and Hexham (twice); however, the handicapper has cut him some slack, and he sits just 1lb higher than when winning readily at Carlisle (2m4f, soft) last November; best form is on soft/heavy.
runners.comment: Modest recent efforts at Chepstow and Hexham; fair mark but best form is on soft/heavy.

runners.name: Dublinofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Suited by the step up in trip when making a winning handicap debut over C&D (good; 16-1) 19 days ago; has more on his plate from this 5lb higher mark but he's low-mileage and could easily be up to the task; major player.
runners.comment: Made winning C&D handicap debut (good; 16-1) 19 days ago; major player from 5lb higher.

runners.name: Takeyoutotheisland
runners.spotlight: Didn't see out his race when seventh on handicap debut at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft; Gonnino second); it's possible he needed that (off a two-month break) but, equally, stamina might have been a factor; 3lb lower and claimer ridden; not impossible.
runners.comment: Didn't see out his race when seventh on handicap debut at Carlisle (2m4f); mark has eased.

runners.name: Mistral Milly
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10); is now rated 6lb lower than when runner-up at Hereford (2m5f, good to soft; for Emma Lavelle) over two years ago; reportedly bled from the nose when fourth of six at Warwick (2m; in an added tongue-tie) seven weeks ago; risks attached.
runners.comment: Reportedly bled from the nose when fourth of six at Warwick (2m; tongue-tie) latest; risky
runners.owner: John Murray & Exor Of Late Niall Farrell

runners.name: Popa Rich
runners.spotlight: Placed on a couple of occasions at Cartmel (2m6f) last year and was a staying-on fifth over C&D (in a first-time tongue-tie) last September; is likely to draw benefit from this comeback run; monitor only for time being.
runners.comment: Staying-on fifth over C&D (added tongue-tie) last September; this is his first run since.

runners.name: Prince Nino
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in 2023 and is considerably lower in the weights now; raced lazily, tailed off, in a division of this last year (good; 5-1); missing from action since so it's tough to know how he'll fare; market check advised.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in 2023; raced lazily, tailed off, in division of this last year; off since.

runners.name: Mayor Of Maghera
runners.spotlight: Lost his way on the Flat for Phil Makin; beaten 44l, 38l, and 80l respectively in three runs over hurdles for this trainer; better ground a plus for handicap debut but only of interest if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Beaten 44l, 38l, & 80l in three runs over hurdles for this trainer; makes handicap debut.

races.race_name: Murrayshall Meetings And Events Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Livano Bello
runners.spotlight: Consistent but continues to seek first win (0-12); produced another decent effort when runner-up at Fakenham (2m4f, good; 11-2) five weeks ago; that form took a slight hit when third-placed Coolkatie was a well-beaten favourite at Uttoxeter subsequently.
