runners.comment: 0-12; another decent effort when runner-up at Fakenham (2m4f, good; 11-2) latest.

runners.name: Beat The Retreat
runners.spotlight: Won two of his last four, accounting for Snow Dragon at Musselburgh (2m4f, soft; from 4lb lower) in February; the snag is that the other two efforts were laboured ones, beaten 28l into fourth over 2m here (good) three weeks ago; this longer trip can assist.
runners.comment: Beaten 28l into fourth over 2m here (good) three weeks ago; the longer trip can assist.

runners.name: Knockeranna Rose
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10); was always to the fore when second-best to Dublinofficecallin (runs in first division at 4.55pm) over C&D recently; fully entitled to be thereabouts if reproducing a similar level; Jack Power takes over in the saddle.
runners.comment: Second-best to Dublinofficecallin (runs in first division) over C&D recently; key player.

runners.name: Theirshegoes
runners.spotlight: No win since November 2024 (Musselburgh, 2m4f) but has slipped to a more manageable mark; lacked for pace when fifth at Hexham (2m, good; 50-1) last month; this longer trip looks a better fit; no back number.
runners.comment: No win since Nov 2024; back on a more manageable mark; lacked for pace over 2m at Hexham.

runners.name: Ivynator
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner for Charlie Fellowes; best effort for Jean McGregor was when runner-up to Follow Charlie in a division of this last year (80-1); autumn form figures of 5PP are hardly a ringing endorsement; easy to swerve off 238-day absence.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a division of this last year (80-1); form figures 05PP since; easy to swerve.

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Veteran; 12-94, four of those wins coming at his beloved Perth; showed up well for much of the contest at Hexham (2m, soft; third, beaten 33l) on Saturday evening, before tiring markedly on the uphill climb; the better ground is in his favour here but this is a quick turnaround.
runners.comment: Tired on the uphill climb at Hexham (2m, soft) on Saturday and this is a quick turnaround.
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Loro White
runners.spotlight: Jumped poorly on hurdles debut at Taunton (2m, heavy) and struggled in two quickfire runs at Hereford (150-1) and Huntingdon (40-1); has undergone wind surgery and reappears at a suitable level for handicap debut in cheekpieces, and on better ground too; expect the market to reveal clues.
runners.comment: Reappears at suitable level for h'cap debut (in cheekpieces); the market can reveal clues.

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in four of seven points; struggled in three runs in maiden/novice company (huge prices) at Ayr, Wetherby, and Hexham; watching brief now back up in trip for handicap debut; his trainer won a division of this last year.
runners.comment: Struggled in three runs in maiden/novice company (huge prices) at Ayr, Wetherby, & Hexham.

runners.name: Jack Dempsey
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; unseated rider on his first two attempts over hurdles and hasn't set the world alight in novice company on last two starts; it's notable that he steps back up in trip for handicap debut (is a half-brother to 2m4f winner Cave Hill) and that should bring about some improvement.
runners.comment: 0-7; steps back up in trip for h'cap debut and that should bring about some improvement.

runners.name: The Longest Day
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back here (3m) in August 2023; 0-10 since and well beaten over 2m when last in action in January; is very lowly weighted if factoring in the 10lb claim of regular rider Roisin Leech.
runners.comment: Won back-to-back here (3m) in August 2023; 0-10 since; well beaten when last seen (2m-3m).

runners.name: Noemie De La Vis
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Compiegne 13 months ago on only start in France; denied in the final strides when 4-7 in a 2m4f mares' maiden at Fairyhouse in January; a lot went wrong for her then, and she looked sure to win before losing vital momentum at the final flight; out of her depth in the Triumph; should be hard to beat here.
runners.comment: Second in races in France and at Fairyhouse, can exploit drop in class after Triumph bid

runners.name: Porcupine Bank
runners.spotlight: Jumping was untidy at times on debut at Tramore last month; owed his third placing to a fall at the last; should improve but may struggle to match the Mullins-trained filly here.
runners.comment: Jumping was untidy at times on debut at Tramore, tough task against the Mullins filly

runners.name: Whitepark Lady
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Yeats Star (hurdle/chase) and Chosen Thyme (bumper); dam fell in a point; Jack Kennedy prefers the stable's once-raced Porcupine Bank.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners, Jack Kennedy prefers the stable's once-raced Porcupine Bank.

runners.name: Out Of Aces
runners.spotlight: Never far from the pace in an academy maiden hurdle at Cork on debut, faded into fourth behind the very useful Quinto Do Lago after a bad mistake at the last; failed to build on that when tailed off at Down Royal in March; stable in superb form on the Flat.
runners.comment: Promising fourth behind a smart type in an academy hurdle, poor run at Down Royal

runners.name: Spellitoutforme
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam placed 2m hurdles, sister to 2m6f hurdle/chase winner Centurion Steel, closely related to high-class bumper and hurdle/chase winner Royal Rendezvous.
runners.comment: Dam a maiden, closely related to bumper/hurdle/chase winner Royal Rendezvous

runners.name: Princedici
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; gelded son of Storm The Stars; dam French 1m1f 2yo winner; likely to need experience.
runners.comment: Gelded son of Storm The Stars, dam French 1m1f 2yo winner, likely to need experience.

runners.name: Matcho d'Agrostis
runners.spotlight: Had already dropped out of contention when falling three out on debut at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Had already dropped out of contention when falling three out on debut at Cork last month

runners.name: Cometothinkofit
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens at Naas and Fairyhouse on his first two starts
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in maidens at Naas and Fairyhouse on his first two starts

runners.name: Salsinha
runners.spotlight: Winning favourite on debut in Cork fillies' bumper last July (good); still looked raw when fourth on heavy ground on her hurdling debut at Navan last November; third in a Listed contest at Thurles the following month; failed to reproduce that form when dropped in class again at Fairyhouse last month; winner ran well on good company at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Bumper winner on good ground last summer, capable of winning a race of of this standard

runners.name: Switch From Diesel
runners.spotlight: Ran in 12 bumpers, winning three and second to Bambino Fever in a Grade 1 at this venue; also 1m4f maiden winner at the Curragh; kept good company in novice hurdles during the winter (last two outings in Grade 1s); might not want the ground too lively but look the part now on her first maiden start since 4l second in a 22-runner contest at Navan last December
runners.comment: Very smart bumper performer, highly tried over hurdles, drops in class with good prospects

runners.name: Aruntothequeen
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner at Gowran in 2024 (2m, good); decent efforts in maiden hurdles at 2m-2m4f; has been found wanting in handicaps, including when aiming high in a valuable mares' event at the DRF; meets Switch From Diesel on 13lb worse than handicap terms.
runners.comment: Listed bumper winner, ordinary hurdles form, rated 13lb inferior to Switch From Diesel
runners.owner: J P O'Flaherty & Mark E Phelan

runners.name: Full Of Shade
runners.spotlight: E280,000 Arqana purchase in 2023 after two runs in France; second at Auteuil was promising but hardly a betting proposition after a three-year absence.
runners.comment: Second at Auteuil in 2023 was promising, best watched after a three-year absence

runners.name: Siofra Beag
runners.spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in five outings on the Flat, all on Dundalk AW.
runners.comment: No sign of worthwhile form in five outings on the Flat, all on Dundalk AW.

runners.name: Cool As A Cat
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m/2m4f hurdle winner for Des McDonogh; 80-1 when unseated rider at the first at Fairyhouse
runners.comment: Dam hurdle winner for Des McDonogh, 80-1 when unseated rider at the first at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Glendaars Crystal
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unplaced in bumper/hurdles, sister to bumper/smart hurdle winner Darlan.
runners.comment: Second foal, dam unplaced in bumper/hurdles, sister to bumper/smart hurdle winner Darlan

runners.name: Valverdeagain
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Poet's Word to Gordon Elliott's useful four-time jumps winner Stuzzikini and hurdles winner Serpolette.
runners.comment: Of some interest as a half-sister to Gordon Elliott's useful four-time winner Stuzzikini

runners.name: Dadou Part Two
runners.spotlight: Always at the back when a 66-1 chance first time out in a bumper at Cork.
runners.comment: Always at the back when a 66-1 chance first time out in a bumper at Cork

runners.name: Claudel
runners.spotlight: No apparent chance in a maiden hurdle at this stage judged on her two bumper outings
runners.comment: No apparent chance in a maiden hurdle at this stage judged on her two bumper outings

runners.name: Money Shuffler
runners.spotlight: Weak form in bumpers and on AW at Dundalk; no hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Weak form in bumpers and on AW at Dundalk, no hurdling experience

runners.name: Sky Lewis
runners.spotlight: Dam was smart in bumpers/Flat; has struggled in two bumpers almost three years apart.
runners.comment: Dam was smart in bumpers/Flat, has struggled in two bumpers almost three years apart

runners.name: Darby Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner is closely related to bumper/chase/hurdle winner St Cuthbert's Cave; 66-1 for last month's hurdling debut over 2m4f at Clonmel, tailed off.
runners.comment: Point winner, 66-1 for last month's hurdling debut over 2m4f at Clonmel, tailed off

runners.name: Chisel Hill
runners.spotlight: Big price when soundly beaten in sixth over a longer trip at this venue on debut; up against it.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in sixth over a longer trip at this venue on debut, up against it

runners.name: Cinnabar Moth
runners.spotlight: Two moderate runs in maiden hurdles since failing to build on a fair first bumper run; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: Failed to build on a fair first bumper run; weak hurdles form, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Barrogstown Girl
runners.spotlight: Progressive on Flat in 2024, winning three handicaps, showing a liking for good ground; two moderate hurdle runs last autumn were on softer going; capable of better now if returning in good shape after a break.
runners.comment: Progressive on Flat in 2024, two moderate hurdle runs last autumn were on softer ground

runners.name: Something Black
runners.spotlight: Weak bumper form; unseated rider at the first on hurdling debut at this venue; down the field at Fairyhouse last November
runners.comment: Unseated rider at the first on hurdling debut, down the field at Fairyhouse last November

runners.name: Turn Up The Heat
runners.spotlight: Rated 81 on Flat after 1m heavy ground win at Leicester in October 2024 on her final start for Ralph Beckett; jumped untidily when a remote sixth in a good mares' maiden hurdle at Fairyhouse 15 months ago; absent since.
runners.comment: Flat winner, jumped untidily at Fairyhouse 15 months ago on her only hurdles start

runners.name: Laurie Blue
runners.spotlight: Won a weak maiden hurdle at Roscommon last August;tdown the field in a handicap on next start; completely out of her depth in a Grade 3 novice chase at Roscommon, and then struggled in lesser company at Thurles.
runners.comment: Won a weak maiden hurdle at Roscommon last August, struggled in two chases before a break

runners.name: Fedneys Park
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner for Mick Goff; last month's Bellewstown fourth provides hope that she can deliver on early promise; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, Bellewstown fourth provides hope that she can deliver on early promise

runners.name: The Border Boy
runners.spotlight: Won 2m1f Cork handicap hurdle last summer on good ground; produced a couple of decent placed efforts after that, including third behind today's rival Hickey's Hill at Thurles; lacks a recent run but ground should suit; on a mark that gives him a fair chance.
runners.comment: Won on good ground at Cork last summer, on a mark that gives him a fair chance

runners.name: Low Style
runners.spotlight: Sole win came back in 2022 (2m, good); lightly raced in recent seasons, managed a good second in 3m Navan handicap chase in February 2025; has been running over longer trips than this over hurdles, well below best on latest.
runners.comment: Sole win came in 2022, has been running over longer trips, well below best on latest

runners.name: Amburana
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame twice at Tramore; beaten 20l in third last time, so needs to improve; previous fourth was better, not beaten far in a race very well advertised by the winner Pebble Bleu and third-placed Cousin Kate; one of the more interesting runners
runners.comment: Has made the frame twice at Tramore, 20l third last time, previous fourth was better

runners.name: Redcliff Glen
runners.spotlight: Limerick handicap hurdle winner by a neck in October 2024 off 94; little of note in his form since then, now down to 89.
runners.comment: Little of note in his form since 2024 but down in the handicap

runners.name: Las Brisas Boy
runners.spotlight: Moderate fourth at Limerick on the first of two handicap runs; made a bad mistake three out at Down Royal last time; will need to improve, tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Moderate fourth on the first of two handicap runs, will need to improve, tongue-tie on now
runners.owner: Anthony Vaughan & Mark Phillips & D P Gaffney

runners.name: Hickeys Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner; well beaten in a bumper and three maiden hurdle outings; left previous track form behind to win at 50-1 over 2m at Fairyhouse last October on handicap debut, with today's rival The Border Boy back in third; unplaced at Clonmel 12 days later and given a break; one for the shortlistt.
runners.comment: One for the shortlist based on a 50-1 win at Fairyhouse on his penultimate 2025 outings

runners.name: Joshsue
runners.spotlight: Has run only twice since making her debut in 2023, first handicap now after 12 months off; rider's claim is a plus factor.
runners.comment: Has run only twice since making her debut in 2023, first handicap now after 12 months off

runners.name: Mount Hotham
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens; 50-1 when in rear at Tramore in his first handicap; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, poor form in maidens, 50-1 when in rear at Tramore in his first handicap

runners.name: Maralinga
runners.spotlight: Two wins on the Flat; modest hurdles form, well beaten in three Thurles outings last October/November; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Two wins on the Flat, modest hurdles form, hard to fancy on return from a break

runners.name: Ghumama
runners.spotlight: 2-39 on Flat, best on AW; best hurdles run when second for Noel Kelly at Downpatrick almost two years ago; ran only once for a different trainer last year; another change of stable now.
runners.comment: 2-39 on Flat, best on AW, best hurdles run when second at Downpatrick almost two years ago

runners.name: Chesterbaylad
runners.spotlight: Attracted support down to 15-2 at Down Royal last week; let down badly by his jumping; may do better, although it is hard to know what inspired the market move, since he has failed to reach the first three in 13 races over hurdles.
runners.comment: Attracted market support at Down Royal last week, let down by his jumping, may do better

runners.name: Hunting Gold
runners.spotlight: Best of her three runs was in an academy hurdle on debut; relatively unexposed, open to improvement.
runners.comment: Best of her three runs was in an academy hurdle, relatively unexposed, open to improvement

runners.name: Joshua Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Absent since August 2023, Tramore win earlier that month was on heavy ground over 2m4f; this trip may be inadequate, and his long absence is a concern.
runners.comment: Absent since August 2023, Tramore win earlier that month was on heavy ground over 2m4f
runners.owner: J R Robinson & Mrs E Keane

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Very weak form, the only positive is that her trainer saddled a long-priced handicap winner last week; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, very weak form, trainer saddled a long-priced handicap winner last week

runners.name: Max Meridius
runners.spotlight: One fair run in a Flat handicap at the Curragh last season, finishing fifth of 25 over 1m2f on heavy ground; hurdles form is uninspiring.
runners.comment: One fair run in a Flat handicap at the Curragh last season, hurdles form is uninspiring

runners.name: Whispering Willow
runners.spotlight: Has had time to mature since her last run; improvement is feasible on first run for Gavin Cromwell.
runners.comment: Has had time to mature since her last run, improvement feasible on first run for this team

runners.name: San Franco
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper; dismal hurdles form in 2025; same story in a Flat outing last October.
runners.comment: Placed in a bumper, dismal hurdles form in 2025, same story in a Flat outing last October

runners.name: Red Veil
runners.spotlight: Placed on the Flat at a modest level; ran poorly in that sphere last month; limited hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Placed on the Flat at a modest level, ran poorly last month, limited hurdling experience

runners.name: Single Edition
runners.spotlight: Lost form for his previous trainer after two good runs in September 2024; plenty to prove on his first run since out of his depth in a Flat maiden last autumn, previously a moderate fourth in a claimer over hurdles.
runners.comment: Lost form for his previous trainer after two good runs in September 2024, no recent form

runners.name: Silent Flight
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner over 2m4f at this venue in January 2024; out of sorts over fences last year; getting on in years now.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner over 2m4f at this venue in January 2024, out of sorts last year

runners.name: Ode To Joy
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been consistent lately; place chance if replicating last month's Bellewstown third behind Bella Union who backed that up with a Down Royal win last week.
runners.comment: Maiden has been consistent lately, place chance if replicating recent Bellewstown effort

runners.name: Spanish Maid
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over hurdles two seasons ago; moderate form over fences until much improved when second at Wexford in March on her stable debut; was well backed then, and recovered losses when even-money favourite at Tramor last month; excellent chance off a similarly low hurdles mark; stable in form.
runners.comment: Winning favourite in a handicap chase at Tramore, leading chance off a similarly low mark

runners.name: Walk On Annilog
runners.spotlight: Two of her three handicap runs have been in much stronger races at Leopardstown last Christmas and Fairyhouse at Easter; this is more realistic, each-way prospect at a decent price.
runners.comment: Two of her three handicap runs have been in much stronger races, this is more realistic

runners.name: Only A Dollar
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 2m Clonmel mares' maiden in 2024; flashes of form last year, placed at Wexford and prospects when falling two out at Clonmel; not a bad run at Limerick after a winter break.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Limerick after a winter break, 1-18 record over hurdles is off-putting

runners.name: Baltinglass Hill
runners.spotlight: Spent most of last season over fences; mid-division over hurdles at Clonmel on latest after being pulled up in consecutive chases; almost two years since the most recent of his three wins.
runners.comment: Spent most of last season over fences, mid-division over hurdles at Clonmel on latest

runners.name: Figurehead
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all categories after 45 races; some of his better runs earlier in his career were over hurdles, placed four times from 18 attempts; a top jockey booked for the trainer's first runner.
runners.comment: Maiden in all categories after 45 races, some of his better runs have been over hurdles

runners.name: Thats All
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 20 starts over hurdles but was generally reliable last summer/autumn; has tended to show her best form over longer distances; win was over 2m3f, second on final start over an extended 2m6f.
runners.comment: Only one win from 20 starts over hurdles but was generally reliable last summer/autumn

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Potentially well handicapped relative to best chase form at Downpatick; fair fifth after long absence at Sligo yesterday; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, potentially well handicapped relative to best chase form, fifth yesterday

runners.name: Urban War
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat early in his career, modest Flat/hurdles form for this stable; fair run at a big price on the Flat at Leopardstown four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won twice on the Flat early in his career, modest Flat/hurdles form for this stable

runners.name: Sekakarma
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maiden hurdles; 50-1 when pulled up on handicap debut at Navan last December.
runners.comment: Weak form in maiden hurdles, 50-1 when pulled up on handicap debut last December

runners.name: Walk Win
runners.spotlight: Hints of promise in maiden hurdle outings at Cork and Bellewstown; hooded now; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, hints of promise in maiden hurdle outings at Cork and Bellewstown, hooded

runners.name: Tippin And Tappin
runners.spotlight: Four hurdle starts were in the second half of 2023; moderate form then, and also in points and over fences in 2024; much-improved last year, winning a point and back-to-back handicaps over fences, both over staying trips; this distance looks inadequate; stable's current form is a positive.
runners.comment: Progressive over fences early last season, stable in form but this trip looks inadequate

runners.name: Ranting And Raven
runners.spotlight: Blinkered when placed three times in middle-distance Flat handicaps on a sound surface for Johnny Murtagh; needs to show major improvement from handicap hurdle debut at Cork;  one of two for the stable.
runners.comment: Placed on the Flat, needs to show major improvement from handicap hurdle debut at Cork

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Reverts to jumps after a snug win over 1m4f at Limerick 18 days ago; relatively unexposed over hurdles and may be capable of improving on previous modest form
runners.comment: Reverts to jumps after a snug win over 1m4f at Limerick, relatively unexposed over hurdles

runners.name: Scottish Reel
runners.spotlight: 60-rated maiden for Charlie Johnston; hurdling debut at Roscommon last August was respectable; two inferior displays after that
runners.comment: Hurdling debut at Roscommon last August was respectable, two inferior displays after that

runners.name: Zarushio
runners.spotlight: Only a small step in the right direction at Bellewstown in his first handicap; well held by third-placed Ode To Joy
runners.comment: Only a small step in the right direction in his first handicap, well held by Ode To Joy

runners.name: Marian Avenue
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of further when staying on over 2m1f at Clonmel in his first handicap; early days, unwise to rule out if any support materialises.
runners.comment: Looked in need of further when staying on over 2m1f at Clonmel, early days, not ruled out

runners.name: Sneddy Eddie
runners.spotlight: Three minor wins on the Flat; has struggled in maiden hurdles; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, three minor wins on the Flat, has struggled in maiden hurdles

runners.name: Soldiers Lady
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form in the final months of 2025, may do better in handicaps on this ground; one of two for the stable.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form in the final months of 2025, may do better in handicaps on this ground

runners.name: Keep It Together
runners.spotlight: No encouragement from her form in four bumpers and four maiden hurdles; in rear on the Flat on final 2025 start.
runners.comment: Weak form in bumpers and maiden hurdles, in rear on the Flat on final 2025 start.

runners.name: Whoswhointhezoo
runners.spotlight: Fair pedigree; showed little in maiden hurdles,; should be more mature now after a break; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Fair pedigree, showed little in maiden hurdles, should be more mature now after a break

runners.name: Flash The Badge
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three maiden hurdles last October/November; opening mark is unappealing.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in three maiden hurdles, opening mark is unappealing

runners.name: Captains Speech
runners.spotlight: Went missing for 704 days after winning his debut Dromahane point-to-point in 2024; returned at Cork (2m4f, yielding) in March when sent off 2-1 joint-favourite and finishing a close runner-up; again filled the runner-up spot at Wexford (2m4f, good to yielding) last time; those runs set the standard.
runners.comment: Point winner sets the standard following runner-up finishes in both hurdle starts

runners.name: Lemmy Caution
runners.spotlight: Cost 250,000GBP after 8l debut point win at Monksgrange last year; bumper form has been nothing special; beaten 34l at Cork last month (2m3f, yielding); respected all the same with Jack Kennedy aboard; declared for 5.05 at Killarney on Monday.
runners.comment: Expensively bought after debut point win but bumper form nothing special; Kennedy rides

runners.name: Nopresentliketime
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; good staying pedigree and was an expensive store; promise in sole bumper and some decent runs in defeat over hurdles without showing much progression; stable second string.
runners.comment: Decent runs over hurdles without showing much progression; second string

runners.name: Vienna Storm
runners.spotlight: Pulled too hard for his Naas heavy ground bumper debut won by better fancied stablemate; stopped quickly that day and tongue tie now goes on; of obvious interest now hurdling given trainer/rider combination.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard for bumper debut; of obvious interest given rider/trainer combo

runners.name: Plastic Paddy
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner in Britain for Michael Appleby; no wins since 2024 and Flat mark has dropped to a basement level; slim hopes rest on hurdles rejuvenating him.
runners.comment: Seven-time AW winner in Britain; no wins since 2024; hopes rest on hurdles sparking him

runners.name: Union Boy
runners.spotlight: Workforce gelding; dam point/2m5f hurdle winner, out of half-sister to bumper winning dam of useful 2m/2m4f hurdle/chase winner Cruising Katie, is granddaughter of useful 2m hurdle winner; trainer 3-137 over hurdles.
runners.comment: Workforce gelding; dam point/2m5f hurdle winner; trainer 3-137 over hurdles

runners.name: Quest For Answers
runners.spotlight: Brother to point winner The Orphan Girl; dam unplaced Flat and jumps, half-sister to winners Screenprint (7f 2yo) and Pastel Blue (1m); purchased for 170,000GBP following 20l success on point-to-point debut at Borris House in an eight-runner field; subsequent form moderate in behind; interesting.
runners.comment: Purchased for 170,000GBP following wide-margin point win; interesting

runners.name: Will Wilde
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning a Sligo bumper in 2023; unseen since finishing fifth in a 3m Leopardstown handicap hurdle in December 2024; best watched after so long away; new headgear.
runners.comment: Bumper winner has been off the track 501 days and is best watched

runners.name: That's It Paddy
runners.spotlight: Fourth on point-to-point debut but pulled up next time when favourite on both occasions; this looks a tough task; beaten a long way 6.58 at Sligo on Tuesday
runners.comment: Fourth on point debut but next two runs uninspiring; this looks a tough task.

runners.name: Papagei
runners.spotlight: Three-time British Flat winner for James Ferguson; no wins since 2024; three maiden hurdle runs for new trainer suggest he is best watched.
runners.comment: No wins since 2024; three m'den hurdle runs for new yard suggest he's best watched

runners.name: The Rebel Fox
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three starts in point-to-points; not a bad run on hurdles debut at Naas, staying on late for a 15l fifth; more needed to win this but open to improvement; place chance.
runners.comment: Placed in all three points; not a bad run when fifth on hurdles bow; place chance

runners.name: Wakey Wakey Man
runners.spotlight: 0-12 under rules' but ran one of his best races when a 3.25l third of 15 at Clonmel (2m7f, yielding) last time, running on well from the rear; up 1lb but this is a lower grade and Kennedy now takes the ride.
runners.comment: Winless bit ran one of his best races when third in a higher grade last time; chance

runners.name: Creadan Grace
runners.spotlight: Upped to 3m for a 20-runner race at Down Royal in October (good to yielding) and it brought improvement for a last gasp win off 82; towards rear throughout when pulled up at Kilbeggan after a break; may come on for that run.
runners.comment: Improved to win at Down Royal in Oct' upped to 3m; PU back from break latest

runners.name: Sheer Raz
runners.spotlight: Has returned from a 405-day absence with three handicap runs, improving with each one; 15-8 favourite at Tramore (2m1f, good to yielding) last time, arriving with every chance before weakening into fifth; this trip is worth a try.
runners.comment: Arrived with every chance at Tramore when weakened into fifth; new trip worth a shot

runners.name: It's Time Again
runners.spotlight: In good form in 2024-2025; placed at Limerick and Gowran before adding a third hurdling win at Ballinrobe (2m6f, good), all three wins coming at that venue; returned from a 378-day absence with an okay run at Kilbeggan last time; could do better with that outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Okay return from over a year off last time; could do better with that run under his belt

runners.name: Ossifer Hops
runners.spotlight: Won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in 2023; modest record overall; 1-17 over hurdles and 0-10 chasing; hard to make a case for on recent form.
runners.comment: Modest record overall with just one win coming in 2023; hard to make a case for

runners.name: The Cobblers Wife
runners.spotlight: Plenty to find based on opening three handicap outings.
runners.comment: Plenty to find based on opening three handicap outings

runners.name: Toor Idaho
runners.spotlight: Totally out of form at the moment; big leap of faith required to back her.
runners.comment: Totally out of form at the moment; big leap of faith required to back her

runners.name: Private Policy
runners.spotlight: Career has been punctuated by long absences; decent effort at Clonmel last time (2m4f, yielding) when left in fourth; this trip could bring improvement.
runners.comment: Decent effort at Clonmel last time when fourth; this trip could bring improvement

runners.name: Ballagh Star
runners.spotlight: Dual winning chaser is rated 8lb lower over hurdles but is 0-14; this ground suits but may need this run to warm up for a summer campaign.
runners.comment: Dual winning chaser is rated 8lb lower over hurdles but is 0-14; may need this run

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; best maiden hurdle effort came when a 20l fifth of 13 at Cork, but was tailed off in a handicap back there three days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; tailed off in a handicap at Cork three days ago
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

runners.name: Schiehallion
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden at the age of nine (0-18); broke a poor run of form back from a break at Bellewstown (3m, yielding) last month when a 1.5l runner-up; that was one of his best runs and chance if backing it up.
runners.comment: 18-race m'den but ran one of his best races when second last time; chance if backing it up

runners.name: Go To Town
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden hurdle runs came at Tipperary in July last year when a 13l fourth of 15; unseen since tailed off at Galway festival later in the summer; makes handicap debut but others preferred.
runners.comment: 13l fourth of 15 at Tipperary was best of three m'den hurdles; others preferred

runners.name: Halsafari
runners.spotlight: First reserve; veteran is a four-time winner; consistent efforts over hurdles at Limerick and Tramore; 3m1f at Cork last time likely stretched him and this trip may do so again.
runners.comment: Reserve; four-time winning veteran who has run consistently in defeat recently
runners.owner: John P Bowe & Michael J Bowe

runners.name: Smackwater Jack
runners.spotlight: Added a second hurdling success over this C&D in June last year off 92 (good to yielding); reverts to hurdles now following several underwhelming runs over fences; time is ticking on but can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Added second hurdles win over C&D last year; reverts to hurdles after poor chase runs

runners.name: Where Are You Now
runners.spotlight: Ran three times in maidens; opening mark proved tempting for punters who made him favourite for a 2m amateur riders' contest over 2m around here (soft to heavy); made a costly error at two out and was only just denied after rallying; up 4lb but this trip may bring further improvement; big player.
runners.comment: Fav' for h'cap debut when denied by a head; new trip may suit; big player

runners.name: Watty Show
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens for Gary McGill and similar story in handicaps in first three starts for this yard; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in runs to date inc' three h'caps; best watched

runners.name: Cullig
runners.spotlight: Winner of a Cork handicap by 20l in March last year (3m1f, good) off 84 and ran well off revised mark subsequently; pulled up at Fairyhouse in November and same story at Clonmel last month; drops into lower grade now but needs to bounce back to form.
runners.comment: 20l winner of h'cap last year but PU last twice; down in grade but bounce back needed
runners.owner: Miss Patricia Coghlan & Diana Edwina Oakes & Guy Culliford

runners.name: Joe's Turn
runners.spotlight: In good form early last year, scoring by a head at Punchestown (3m, yielding) off 80; beaten a neck off revised mark at Thurles next time (3m1f, good to yielding); fell at the fifth at Kilbeggan when last seen in April; has tended to improve with a run in the past, but player if returning in the same form.
runners.comment: In good form early last year; may need the run but player if returning in same form

runners.name: Elbow Lane
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three maiden hurdles; 66-1 for handicap debut at Clonmel when a 17.5l seventh of 13; others preferred.
runners.comment: Tailed off in m'dens; 66-1 for h'cap debut when well beaten

runners.name: Illtakehimmyself
runners.spotlight: Stayed on for a distant fifth of 13 on handicap debut at Limerick in December (2m3f, soft to heavy); this step up in trip may help; tongue tie goes on.
runners.comment: Stayed on for a distant fifth on h'cap; step up in trip may help

runners.name: Dearg Mor
runners.spotlight: 200-1 for all three maidens and ran accordingly; tailed off on handicap debut over 2m3f around here; hopes rest on new headgear and new type of ground transforming him.
runners.comment: Tailed off on h'cap debut last time; hopes rest on new h'gear/new ground transforming him

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back h'cap chase winner in October on good to yielding; unable to take advantage of lower hurdles mark in three recent runs over hurdles; probably wouldn't have done better than fourth when faller at Clonmel last time; has his chance again but inability to win over timber to date is worrying.
runners.comment: Two chase wins in Oct'; unable to take advantage of lower hurdle mark in three recent runs
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Journey So Far
runners.spotlight: Winless from nine starts under rules' and yet to get any closer than 22l to the winner; longer trip and better ground here but hard to fancy on form.
runners.comment: Yet to get any closer than 22l to the winner in nine starts under rules'

runners.name: Orlando Spirit
runners.spotlight: Good runs in defeat over 2m1f at Thurles on soft to heavy (keeping on and beaten a neck) and when a distant third at Cork (2m1f, yielding); tailed off at Limerick last time, but he's interesting now upped to this trip.
runners.comment: Some good runs in defeat over shorter trips when showing stamina; interesting over 3m now

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: 0-21 over hurdles and tailed off in latest two starts in this sphere at turn of the year; decent chasing debut at Wexford when third but pulled up at Tramore last time after making a bad mistake.
runners.comment: 0-21 over hurdles; made decent chasing debut but PU at Tramore latest after bad mistake

runners.name: Mickey Hulie
runners.spotlight: Dual winning point-to-pointer is yet to score from nine runs under rules; claims on the basis of his neck runner-up finish at Naas (3m, soft to heavy); below that level when favourite at Limerick eight days later; cheekpieces worn at Kilbeggan last time when again disappointing are now left off.
runners.comment: Claims on basis of neck second at Naas but has disappointed in two runs since

runners.name: Written In My Soul
runners.spotlight: Weakened into sixth of 18 at Down Royal on handicap debut in March (2m5f, yielding); returns to this sphere following two poor efforts over fences.
runners.comment: Weakened into sixth of 18 on h'cap debut; reverts to timber following two poor chase runs

runners.name: Coffeys Forge
runners.spotlight: Good ground winning chaser is 0-19 over hurdles; ran well off this mark at Clonmel (2m4f, heavy) in January when a 0.5l runner-up; brought down at the third at Tramore in a first-time tongue tie (discarded) last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Winning chaser is 0-19 over hurdles; ran well off this mark in Jan'; BD latest

runners.name: Annie Button
runners.spotlight: Carries a 7lb penalty courtesy of her breakthrough win by a head at Cork three days ago (3m1f, good); that was her first run over 3m on better ground and in cheekpieces, so those may work their magic once again.
runners.comment: Penalised 7lb after making the breakthrough last time; big player

runners.name: Ballinaboola Gold
runners.spotlight: Down the field in opening three handicaps over various trips and ground; has time on her side but hard to make a case for at the moment.
runners.comment: Down the field in three h'caps over various trips and ground; hard to make a case for

runners.name: Queen Kulina
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran some good runs in defeat back from over a year off towards back end of last year; more needed to win here.
runners.comment: M'den ran some good runs in defeat towards back end of last year; more needed

runners.name: Thatsmyroadie
runners.spotlight: First reserve; pulled up on handicap debut at Kilbeggan when 66-1; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Reserve; pulled up on handicap debut at Kilbeggan when 66-1; hard to fancy

runners.name: True Harmony
runners.spotlight: Won his second point-to-point on good to yielding ground but struggled four maiden hurdles on winter ground; worth a market check for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won a point on gd/yld; struggled in four m'dens on winter ground; check market

runners.name: Johnny M
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three 2024 handicaps for Peter Fahey; can only be watched back from such a long absence; makes stable debut in first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Poor form in 2024 and can only be watched returning from such a long absence

runners.name: Max Time
runners.spotlight: Galway win in September 2024 was a flash in the pan; unplaced in 31 of his 32 starts; beaten 27l at Thurles when last seen in January.
runners.comment: Galway win in Sept' 2024 was a flash in the pan; unplaced in 31 of 32 starts

runners.name: Rideau Canal
runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark at Thurles in February in an amateur handicap chase (soft to heavy), winning going away on only his third run over fences; reverts to hurdles after pulled up at Limerick last time and record of 0-21 is off-putting.
runners.comment: Belatedly off mark over fences in Feb'; reverts to hurdles but 0-21 record is off-putting

runners.name: Lissen To The Lady
runners.spotlight: Seven-time hurdles winner made a satisfactory return from a long absence at Naas when sixth; didn't build on that at Clonmel last time; others preferred.
runners.comment: Made a satisfactory return from a long absence at Naas but didn't build on that last time

runners.name: Urbs Intacta Manet
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden form and didn't show improvement for switch to handicaps at Down Royal last month; cheekpieces go on but unlikely.
runners.comment: Poor m'den form and didn't show improvement for switch to h'caps last time

runners.name: Clandara
runners.spotlight: Well beaten back from a long absence at Wexford in October (2m4f, yielding); tongue tie goes on but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Well beaten back from absence at Wexford in Oct'; others more appealing

runners.name: Walking In Mayo
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of her only point to point; improved over moderate maiden runs when sixth over C&D on final start for Philip Rothwell (heavy); tailed off here on stable debut last time (2m7f, heavy).
runners.comment: Easy point winner ran okay on C&D h'cap debut but tailed off here last time

runners.name: The Cheeky Pup
runners.spotlight: No signs of winning potential in two maiden hurdles or a Galway festival novice contest; makes h'cap debut now.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in three runs to date; makes h'cap debut now

runners.name: Emerald Enigma
runners.spotlight: Debut ladies' bumper winner at Cork by 0.75l on yielding; rider felt she would improve a lot for the experience; big chance of following up
runners.comment: Debut bumper winner and rider felt she would improve a lot; big chance of follow up

runners.name: Working Class Hero
runners.spotlight: Had a subsequent winner behind when third in a Dromahane point on debut for Luke Murphy in November 2024; pulled up at Borris House the following month; good rules debut over C&D 11 days ago when a distant second; likely to be seen to best effect when stepped up in trip.
runners.comment: Distant second on C&D rules' debut; likely his optimum will be over further

runners.name: Mila Ballerina
runners.spotlight: Australia mare; closely related to 1m2f-1m4f Flat (inc Listed; RPR 111) and high-class 2m hurdle winner Jason The Militant, half-sister to useful 2m3f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Will Carver; dam 2m hurdle winner, out of useful 1m2f-1m4f winner; interesting pedigree and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Australia mare; closely related to quality Flat/hurdler Jason The Militant; check market

runners.name: Ballito Beauty
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m2f hurdle winner, half-sister to useful bumper and 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase winner Whatswrongwithyou, out of unraced half-sister to useful bumper/2m hurdle winner No Where To Hyde; likely best watched for her debut.
runners.comment: Dam 2m2f hurdle winner; likely best watched for her debut

runners.name: Crios Cu Chulainn
runners.spotlight: Idaho mare; dam fourth in bumper, half-sister to Champion Hurdle winner Faugheen, out of unraced half-sister to 2m1f-2m6f chase winner Trotsky; trainer 0-29 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Dam 4th in bumper, half-sister to Champion Hurdle winner Faugheen; trainer 0-29 in bumpers

runners.name: Coolnagrattan
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word gelding; dam 9.5f/10.4f winner (RPR 75), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Bravo Zolo, out of 6f 2yo/7f winner; headgear combination suggests he's a keen-going sort.
runners.comment: Dam 9.5f/10.4f winner (RPR 75); headgear combo suggests he's a keen-going sort

runners.name: My Uncle Jack
runners.spotlight: Dam unraced half-sister to winners Gerri Colombe (bumper and top-class 2m3f-3m2f hurdle/chase) and La Jubanaise (French 11.5f-1m6f), out of maiden half-sister to useful French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Garde Chasse; trainer is 2-3 in bumpers and top amateur aboard so check market.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to two winners inc' Gerri Colombe; trainer 2-3 in bumpers

runners.name: Coolishael
runners.spotlight: Fourth in his opening point run and bettered that when a 2l third 15 days later; needs to improve again to be involved here.
runners.comment: Fourth in opening point and bettered that when third next time; needs to improve again

runners.name: An Bhoireann
runners.spotlight: Modest point form and tailed off at 100-1 on bumper debut at Bellewstown; ruled out.
runners.comment: Modest point form and tailed off at 100-1 on bumper debut at Bellewstown; ruled out

runners.name: Daily Devotion
runners.spotlight: Always behind on bumper debut at Gowran when tailed off on heavy ground; others preferred.
runners.comment: Always behind on bumper debut at Gowran when tailed off on heavy ground; others preferred

runners.name: Dark Eagle
runners.spotlight: Related to a couple of winners but hasn't landed a blow in two bumpers to date, the latest at 100-1.
runners.comment: Related to a couple of winners but hasn't landed a blow in two bumpers to date

runners.name: Mister Killeens
runners.spotlight: Beaten over 20l in both academy hurdles and similar story at Naas on bumper debut; much more needed.
runners.comment: Beaten over 20l in both academy hurdles and on bumper debut; much more needed

runners.name: Tale Of Intrigue
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 80-1 for his bumper debut at the festival here 11 days ago when tailed off; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 80-1 for bumper debut here 11 days ago when tailed off; hard to fancy

runners.name: Mr Brentin
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in opening bumper run at Ballinrobe and now has a 295-day absence to overcome.
runners.comment: Tailed off in opening bumper run at Ballinrobe and now has a 295-day absence to overcome

races.race_name: Classic Car Show On 9th June Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lirone Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: French 2m2f hurdle runner-up on very soft; finished weakly and tailed off in his two races for Dan Skelton who now turns to cheekpieces; it would be disappointing if he's not capable of better at some stage.
runners.comment: French runner-up; quiet in two runs for this yard but should be capable of a lot better

runners.name: Arctic Voyage
runners.spotlight: 6f winner for Ralph Beckett and came good at the sixth attempt over hurdles with a clearcut win at Hereford (2m, good; hood removed); this 4yo now carries a penalty but has less to prove than most of these.
runners.comment: Penalised for his clearcut Hereford win but has less to prove than most of these

runners.name: Arbitration
runners.spotlight: Won over 9.4f on the AW last September for Karl Burke, his only win in 12 outings; of useful ability and he fetched 67,000gns at the sales.
runners.comment: Of useful ability on the Flat and he fetched 67,000gns at the sales

runners.name: Gone Away Madam
runners.spotlight: Clearcut winner of a Uttoxeter bumper (good) but didn't really build on that, including her hurdle debut when last seen two years ago; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but off for two years and probably best watched

runners.name: Bontessony Doll
runners.spotlight: Second foal out of a 2m2f hurdle winner and half-sister to useful 2m6f-3m3f hurdle/chase winner Geeveem; can only be watched on what is a very belated debut.
runners.comment: Out of a hurdle winner but can only be watched on what is a very belated debut

runners.name: Blue Siam
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden and insufficient promise in her two races over hurdles to consider.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden and insufficient promise in her two races over hurdles to consider

runners.name: Louis Des Landes
runners.spotlight: Unseated early at Wincanton before suffering a heavy defeat on good ground at Chepstow; opposable.
runners.comment: Unseated early at Wincanton before suffering a heavy defeat on good ground at Chepstow

runners.name: Jubertilee
runners.spotlight: Multiple point winner but beaten about 30l in his two races over hurdles, the latest last week.
runners.comment: Multiple point winner but beaten 30l in his two races over hurdles, the latest last week

runners.name: Keep Off The Sugar
runners.spotlight: Off for 339 days after showing some ability in his first bumper; completely tailed off in two others for this yard (hooded last time).
runners.comment: Completely tailed off in two bumpers for this yard (hooded last time)

runners.name: Medina Blue
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a bumper in Ireland where she was runner-up in two points; joined this yard for 19,000euros; wouldn't be the obvious solution here.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a bumper in Ireland where she was runner-up in two points

races.race_name: Day Before Terry Wheeler's Birthday Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Recently changed hands for 8,000gns after a quiet run on the Flat; did win over hurdles for Brian Ellison and was second off this mark at Sedgefield last month; could feature.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles for Brian Ellison and was second off this mark at Sedgefield last month

runners.name: Reel Orange
runners.spotlight: In this headgear combination for the first time when winning by a length over 2m1f here last month (good) and then beat all bar a Skelton-trained favourite off this 3lb higher mark; looks sure to go well.
runners.comment: First and second in her last two handicaps, both here on good ground; solid
runners.owner: J M Dare, T Hamlin, J W Snook

runners.name: Yanka Blue
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has shown nothing in 2m4f/2m7f handicaps for her current yard; tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Maiden who has shown nothing in 2m4f/2m7f handicaps for current yard; tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: An improved second from the front at Uttoxeter (2m, good) where the winner was much too good; handicap newcomers from this yard are always of interest.
runners.comment: Better run last time and handicap newcomers from this yard are always of interest

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has won a handicap (off 6lb lower) but there are long absences in her profile and she was pulled up when returning from one at Fontwell in March.
runners.comment: Handicap winner but was pulled up in March when returning from another absence

runners.name: Isabella Islay
runners.spotlight: It was a modest maiden for mares that she won at Hereford (2m3f, good) but that was still a sizeable step forward on her previous efforts on slower ground; open to more improvement now handicapping.
runners.comment: First run on good ground when flooring the favourite in a maiden for mares at Hereford

runners.name: Miss Pearl
runners.spotlight: Very nearly won a handicap at Bangor early last year and was placed again next time; however, her form ran cold afterwards and she's been off for 13 months; new trainer.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature off this mark but still a maiden and absent for 13 months

runners.name: Georgia's Charm
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers for Harry Fry; hasn't got a lot going at all for current yard but handicaps are now a better option; steps up in trip as well.
runners.comment: Weak form claims but handicaps are now a better option; steps up in trip as well

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Inca De Lafayette
runners.spotlight: Only his third run over fences when strolling home in a small field at Plumpton (2m4f, good); hit with a 7lb rise but his last win over hurdles was off only 3lb lower; good chance.
runners.comment: Only his third run over fences when strolling home in a small field at Plumpton (2m4f)

runners.name: Anyharminasking
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who made it 0-5 over fences with another disappointing show at Bangor in late March, despite returning to good ground; clearly needs to raise his game in the new blinkers (replacing cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Three-time hurdle winner but chase profile has a regressive look to it; tries blinkers

runners.name: Siam Park
runners.spotlight: He's 4-8 over fences and fell in two of the defeats, including last time when he was still travelling nicely four from home; likely to be bang there if avoiding the mistakes.
runners.comment: Four wins over fences; still going well when falling four out here the last time

runners.name: Move With The Beat
runners.spotlight: Multiple point winner who is now on a roll over fences, making it three wins in his last four starts when doing it comfortably at Warwick (2m4f, good); the 4lb rise seems fair and he's clicked with Isabelle Ryder.
runners.comment: Won three of his last four and has clicked with Isabelle Ryder; latest 4lb rise very fair

races.race_name: Come Racing On 27th May Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cobbler's Boy
runners.spotlight: By no means an instant success over fences but came good in a small field here last week (good); returns to hurdles off the very same mark and he did win a maiden in this discipline.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner; last week's easy chase victory came off this very mark

runners.name: Blacksamssenorita
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner; her jumping left a bit to be desired back over hurdles in new blinkers at Exeter a month ago (2m, good; no match for the 8l winner) and Brendan Powell presumably prefers the chances of stablemate Ellerton.
runners.comment: Jumping left a bit to be desired back over hurdles in new blinkers at Exeter a month ago

runners.name: One Island
runners.spotlight: Won a Flat race in France; beaten between 15l and 25l in three non-handicaps in Britain, but it's still early days and cheekpieces go on for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won a Flat race in France; beaten between 15l and 25l in three non-handicaps in Britain

runners.name: Ellerton
runners.spotlight: First run for Joe Tizzard when justifying market confidence at Wincanton (2m4f, good) in March last year and unlikely he gave his true running when well beaten after a 7lb rise at Taunton in November; could be given another chance, though lengthy breaks have become a feature in his profile.
runners.comment: Winning debut for this yard and unlikely he gave his true running in November

runners.name: Stinginhisstep
runners.spotlight: Course regular who was mostly in good form last year and last month's reappearance run at Exeter looked too bad to be true; hard to dismiss, especially back here.
runners.comment: Course regular who was mostly in good form last year; Exeter return too bad to be true
runners.owner: A Leach And D V Gardner

races.race_name: St. Austell Brewery Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hoe Joly Smoke
runners.spotlight: Ran really well in defeat in some strong Cheltenham handicaps (2m4f-3m1f) during the first half of last season and his Kempton effort in February wasn't too shoddy; what needs forgiving is his Ayr run after a wind operation as that was underwhelming; still looks dangerous.
runners.comment: His Ayr run after wind surgery was underwhelming but massive shout on his peak form

runners.name: Viroflay
runners.spotlight: Has had no trouble in transferring his useful hunter chase form to handicaps, last season winning at Kempton (3m, good) and runner-up there (2m4f, good to soft) in February; his Topham run can be forgiven and, while he might be better going right-handed, he could be tough to catch around here.
runners.comment: A bold-jumping front-runner who could be tough to catch around here if on his game

runners.name: Lord Baddesley
runners.spotlight: Rather lost his way last season after winning at Newbury in October and last week's Fakenham run was an all-time low; tries a visor this time, but blinkers and cheekpieces did nothing for him.
runners.comment: Seems to have lost his way markedly with last week's run an all-time low; more new aids

runners.name: Certainly Red
runners.spotlight: Missed all of 2025 but he ran encouragingly on belated reappearance at Sandown (3m, soft; 50-1) two months ago, still disputing the lead when making a mess of the second last; fine on this ground and has to be considered.
runners.comment: Bad late mistake was no help at Sandown and that was an encouraging return

races.race_name: Totnes And Bridgetown Chase Company Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Golden Son
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper; easy winner of two hunter chases before a commendable fourth in the big one at the Cheltenham Festival; he was only 17-2 for the Aintree version but never emerged from midfield; entitled to take care of these.
runners.comment: Entitled to take care of these on the strength of his fourth at Cheltenham in March
runners.owner: G Mason, A Ferguson And P Nicholls

runners.name: Regatta De Blanc
runners.spotlight: Made it 6-7 in points with recent short-priced wins over 3m and 4m, and she's won hunter chases at Taunton and Cheltenham; appeals as the main danger to Golden Son.
runners.comment: Dual hunter chase winner who made it 6-7 in points with two wins in April

runners.name: High Game Royal
runners.spotlight: Beaten between 17l and 33l in his last three hunter chases, getting outpaced at trips from 2m5f to 3m1f; tongue-tie is now added; probably playing for minor money.
runners.comment: Beaten between 17l and 33l in his last three hunter chases, from 2m5f to 3m1f

runners.name: Get Off Your Phone
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-16 in points and still had a chance when slipping up in the latest; however, balance of form in that scene gives her lots to find at this level.
runners.comment: Balance of form in the pointing scene (3-16) gives her lots to find at this level

runners.name: Est Illic
runners.spotlight: Recent point winner but a modest performer under rules these days and his Ludlow third was remote.
runners.comment: A modest performer under rules these days and his Ludlow third was remote

runners.name: Fongs Way
runners.spotlight: This is a belated first run over regulation fences and he's only 1-15 in points; looks outclassed.
runners.comment: A belated first run over regulation fences and only 1-15 in points; looks outclassed

races.race_name: William Hill Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lundy View
runners.spotlight: Made up ground from off the pace to go down by just under 10l on debut here (33-1); possibilities with a step forward.
runners.comment: Made up ground from off the pace to go down by just under 10l on debut here

runners.name: Cbils Moon
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a point bumper in March and couldn't recommend on the back of that.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a point bumper in March and couldn't recommend on the back of that

runners.name: Holy Cassia
runners.spotlight: Dam was fourth in a bumper and half-sister to bumper and high-class 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Midnight Shadow; stable 0-10 in bumpers recent seasons.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a yard that's 0-10 in bumpers during recent seasons

runners.name: Milliedram
runners.spotlight: £1,500 3yo; half-sister to winners Gaitway (bumper/2m hurdle), Diamond Gait (bumper/2m hurdle) and Commander Miller (2m3f hurdle); dam 7f 2yo winner; yard 0-15 in bumpers the last five seasons; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: £1,500 3yo; yard 0-15 in bumpers the last five seasons; look elsewhere

runners.name: Newbridge
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to five winners including Perfect Pirate (useful 2m4f chase/2m5f hurdle), River Arrow (2m4f hurdle), Lone Star (2m4f/2m7f hurdle) and Pembridge (2m6f hurdle); dam placed in a 2m4f hurdle; bred to have a future.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners so bred to have a future; stable does okay in bumpers

runners.name: Flora Hobbs
runners.spotlight: Hard to know what she achieved in winning a recent point bumper on good ground but she did it easily by 8l; clearly has ability.
runners.comment: A recent point bumper winner on good ground and took that by some 8l

runners.name: Moonlight Ballet
runners.spotlight: Unraced dam out of 1m Listed winning half-sister to 1m2f-2m winner Northern Frontier; stable hasn't sent out a bumper winner in five years, so probably one for another day.
runners.comment: Stable hasn't sent out a bumper winner in five years, so one for another day

runners.name: Unflappable
runners.spotlight: £4,500 3yo; third foal; half-sister to 2m-2m1f chase winner Pure Carbon; dam well held in points; trainer gets occasional success in these races so it makes sense to check the betting.
runners.comment: £4,500 3yo; third foal; half-sister to 2m-2m1f chase winner Pure Carbon

runners.name: Cantcallherthat
runners.spotlight: 40-1 when only seventh on good ground at Hereford, trailing the winner by 24l; this looks weaker than that race.
runners.comment: 40-1 when only seventh on good ground at Hereford, trailing the winner by 24l

runners.name: Mirabello
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on soft-ground debut; showed more on good at Plumpton when fourth of seven and beaten under 5l; did bleed from the nose that day but likely contender here.
runners.comment: Showed more on good at Plumpton when fourth of seven and beaten under 5l

races.race_name: Visit fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Anglesey Lad
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced after ten months off when fifth of ten in 5f Southwell AW handicap four weeks ago; he's 0-20 however.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after ten months off with Southwell fifth four weeks ago; he's 0-20 though

runners.name: Darkened Edge
runners.spotlight: C&D scorer who has not been seen since beating just one (stumbled halfway) in 5f Lingfield handicap in September; she needs to hit the ground running.
runners.comment: C&D scorer but beat just one at Lingfield in September; needs to hit the ground running
runners.owner: Mrs N Davies And Chris Mason

runners.name: Wedgewood
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways on Lingfield AW (5f) in March and backed it up with a very good second of five in another 5f handicap there 13 days ago; this C&D scorer merits serious consideration.
runners.comment: Good second on Lingfield AW 13 days ago; this C&D scorer merits serious consideration

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner but yet to fire this term, finishing only seventh of nine in 5f Wolverhampton AW handicap last month; has run well in these aids in the past.
runners.comment: Dual course winner but yet to fire this term; has run well in these aids in the past

runners.name: Payforanother Daay
runners.spotlight: Remains winless; off since coming in last (badly hampered) in 5.5f handicap here in October so has itness to prove after seven months off.
runners.comment: Remains winless; has his fitness to prove after seven months off

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and she has failed to beat a rival in 5f/6f Southwell handicaps on her last two runs; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in Southwell AW h'caps on her last two runs; easy to look elsewhere

runners.name: Nordic Glory
runners.spotlight: A three-time 6f AW winner in 2025 and in good nick over the winter; just as effective on turf and this quirky sort is not out of things back from a break today.
runners.comment: In good nick over the winter; this quirky sort is not out of things back from a break

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: On a long losing sequence and she failed to beat a rival in a 6f Salisbury handicap ten days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On a long losing sequence and she failed to beat a rival in Salsibury h'cap ten days ago
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Up The Anti
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 1m Kempton AW win but yet to hit top form this season, beating just one at Kempton (6f) last month; this course scorer needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top form this season and this course scorer needs to bounce back

races.race_name: Fairplay Let's Bet On It! British EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: El Lambo
runners.spotlight: Coulsty colt who showed modest form when sixth in 5f Newbury maiden and fifth in 6f Lingfield novice last month; he needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: Modest form shown in his two runs last month and he needs to take a step forward
runners.owner: The Lamb Inn Wantage Racing Team

runners.name: Romeo Guest
runners.spotlight: £26,000 yearling; Bungle Inthejungle colt who is a half-brother to four winners, notably Miss Attitude (5f Listed; RPR 112); dam, 5f winner (RPR 87); appeals on paper and a likely player for stable among the winners, including with its juveniles.
runners.comment: Bungle Inthejungle colt who appeals on paper and rates a likely player on his debut

runners.name: Time Saxon Warrior
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros yearling; Saxon Warrior colt; half-brother to winners Winding Stream (7f; RPR 91) and Spycraft (7f; RPR 70); dam, unraced half-sister to 7f winner Never Better, is out of a sister to Group 1 winners Magical (1m2f-1m4f) and Rhododendron (1m-1m2f); this debutant must enter calculations, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Saxon Warrior colt; debutant enters calculations, especially if market vibes are positive

runners.name: Tribal Appearance
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling; half-brother to four winners, notably Belle Image (6f; RPR 106) and Isabeau (Listed-placed 5f; RPR 100); dam, placed at 7f (RPR 65); yard has already had a couple of juvenile winners this season so this Sioux Nation colt needs considering.
runners.comment: Yard already had two juvenile winners in 2026 so this Sioux Nation colt needs considering

runners.name: Martha Brown
runners.spotlight: 50-1, finished last of nine in 6f Kempton novice on her debut nine days ago; significantly more is required from this Aclaim filly.
runners.comment: Last of nine in Kempton novice on her debut; significantly more is required

races.race_name: Platinum Motor Group Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Darzah
runners.spotlight: 14-1, promising debut fifth of eight in maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) four weeks ago, when prominent to 1f out; that form has been franked so this daughter of Dubawi rates a major player.
runners.comment: Promising debut fifth at Newmarket; form been franked so she rates a major player

runners.name: Vitality
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly; shaped with some encouragement when placed at around 1m in a pair of novices at Southwell and Epsom last month; she may do better still so can't be ruled out for yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Placed in pair of novices at Southwell and Epsom last month so can't be ruled out
runners.owner: Miss D Finkler, A Herd, Dr P Holloway 1

runners.name: Mobberley Rose
runners.spotlight: 11-4, easily landed an 8.5f Wolverhampton AW novice on her belated debut in March, drawing clear final furlong; she has to concede weight all round but is still not taken lightly.
runners.comment: Easy winner on Wolverhampton AW debut; concedes weight all round but not taken lightly

runners.name: Kaiko
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly; half-sister to winners Laabbij (Qatari 6f) and Spaggiari (Polish 1m); dam, unraced half-sister to Listed winners Marquix (1m) and Sceptre Rouge (7f 2yo), out of 1m Listed winner; market can guide on debut.
runners.comment: Kameko filly; the market can guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Curtain Caller
runners.spotlight: Has beaten one rival in 7f novices at Southwell and Lingfield this spring; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Has beaten one rival in 7f novices at Southwell and Lingfield this spring

runners.name: Whisper Of Mist
runners.spotlight: Has offered little in a pair of 1m Kempton novices this winter; lots more is required after a break.
runners.comment: Has offered little in a pair of 1m Kempton novices this winter; lots more required

runners.name: Foothold
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he's proving a reliable sort and not seen to best effect when fifth of seven in 1m2f Goodwood handicap 12 days ago; not out of things off a slipping mark.
runners.comment: Not seen to best effect with Goodwood fifth latest; not out of things from easing mark

runners.name: Fizzy Bear
runners.spotlight: Son of Kodiac who has been brought along steadily; patiently ridden fifth of nine in 8.5f Epsom novice 15 days ago; looks to have more to offer now going handicapping; interesting.
runners.comment: Patiently ridden fifth at Epsom latest; more to offer now h'capping; interesting

runners.name: Sup Of Red
runners.spotlight: In good form without winning this season until just seventh of 12 in 7f Kempton handicap 13 days ago; she's the sort to bounce back though.
runners.comment: In good form until seventh at Kempton 13 days ago; she's the sort to bounce back though

runners.name: Halla Bil Zain
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m maiden last year for Owen Burrows; gelded and yet to offer much for his new yard this spring though, beating only one over C&D last time; others are much preferred.
runners.comment: Yet to offer much for new yard this spring, beating only one over C&D last time

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough win on his return at Chelmsford (1m) in March but below par more recently; well-held third of six in 1m2f Redcar handicap last time; blinkers are reached for.
runners.comment: Well-held third of six in 1m2f Redcar handicap last time; blinkers are reached for

runners.name: Phantom Shadow
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in three runs last term; gelded before coming in last of eight on his handicap debut/return at Yarmouth (1m) last month so has a bit to prove here.
runners.comment: Last of eight on h'cap debut/return at Yarmouth last month so has a bit to prove

runners.name: Quilt
runners.spotlight: Ardad filly; made a promising debut when fourth in 7f Kempton novice in October but she failed to build on it in 7f maidens at Newcastle and Lingfield following month; returns for her handicap debut and could still do better for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Promising Kempton fourth but failed to build on it; could still do better on h'cap debut

races.race_name: Best Cleaning Supporting #mentalhealthawarenesswe Handicap (Bath Summer Series Stayers' Qualifier)

runners.name: Endofastorm
runners.spotlight: In-form mare whose record at Bath features wins in 2024/2025 and a couple of second-place finishes (over 11.6f/1m2f) since returned to turf from AW, the penultimate occasion behind Moon Over The Sea; weighted to reverse the placings; good chance.
runners.comment: Better off with Moon Over The Sea on C&D running two starts ago; good chance

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: Contrasting efforts over C&D on last two starts, finishing a respectable third to Moon Over The Sea then below-par fifth to Virtual Hug; still a maiden and his chance of finally winning partly depends on the effects of first-time eyeshield.
runners.comment: Chance of opening his account depends on the effects of the eyeshield

runners.name: Basilette
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner; only a modest third at Salisbury (1m6f) on reappearance and belated return to the Flat but gives the impression she may do better back down in distance with the outing under her belt.
runners.comment: Maiden on the Flat but may step up on Salisbury reappearance effort

runners.name: Virtual Hug
runners.spotlight: Belatedly opened his Flat turf account in similar event over C&D (good) last week, taking Bath record to 42321 and adding to his AW/hurdles wins; incurs 4lb penalty but defied this mark a couple of years ago; very solid chance.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last week, taking Bath record to 42321; very solid chance
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

runners.name: Lady Aiyana
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden for the Kublers in her first season last year and was campaigned mostly on AW; ran well on handicap debut but failed to back up that effort; still with same owner; tongue-tie fitted; enough to prove back on turf.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden last term for previous yard; tongue-tie fitted

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: Brings good stats, being 2-2 since returned to turf from AW and 2-3 at Bath; justified favouritism at Windsor last week when avoiding a penalty for his C&D success under Myla Coppins; has to deal with revised mark and a 4lb penalty but still respected.
runners.comment: Brings good stats, being 2-2 since returned to turf and 2-3 at Bath; respected

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 Free At fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Kakirra
runners.spotlight: Hooded on first three starts but not when upped markedly in trip for his handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; 16-1) five days ago, completing the new look when he showed his first worthwhile form and won a seven-runner race by just over 4l, coming from the back; has 6lb penalty for this turf debut but is of major interest now that he's turned over a new leaf.
runners.comment: Has a 6lb penalty today but he won well at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; 16-1) five days ago

runners.name: Montu
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; placed three times in a row in February/March in AW handicaps from 9.4f to 1m4f; latest start (second-time cheekpieces) was not as good and this return to turf (first time since debut) asks another question but he's still considered in a weak race.
runners.comment: 0-8; placed three times in February/March in AW handicaps; latest start was not so good

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: Wore cheekpieces at Yarmouth on last two outings, on first occasion getting up late on over 1m (good to firm); good third of ten when favourite one week later was over 1m2f (good); raised another 2lb but brings some relatively solid form, something which many of his rivals lack; major player.
runners.comment: Registered 1m win and a good 1m2f third at Yarmouth in his last two starts; major player

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: 0-8 last season and he's not seriously threatened to put that right in his three runs this year; wore cheekpieces on six of his last seven starts, blinkers in the other, but now tries a hood on first start since leaving the Russell/Scudamore yard; also steps up from 8.6f.
runners.comment: 0-11; tries a hood on first start since leaving the Russell/Scudamore yard; up from 8.6f

runners.name: Atalanta Mist
runners.spotlight: Debut was much the best of three runs as 2yo; more like it again in this season's two handicaps, both over 1m here and particularly last time (firm) when always-prominent third of seven; sire suggests she will stay 1m2f; 5lb claimer is recruited; probably thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Third of seven here (1m) last time; sire suggests she'll stay 1m2f; 5lb claimer recruited

runners.name: Favourite Friend
runners.spotlight: Probably bred for further than this but, from a leading role over 1f out, he weakened into sixth of 11 on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW) 16 days ago; 16-1 that day and cheekpieces are now called on.
runners.comment: 16-1, weakened late into 6th of 11 on recent handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW)

runners.name: Skyolaire
runners.spotlight: There has twice been a whiff of ability from her four starts, including in fourth of seven here (1m, firm) on handicap debut two weeks ago when she finished on the heels of Atalanta Mist; both have been dropped just 1lb; dam 1m2f/1m4f winner, so this step up in trip might spark some improvement.
runners.comment: 4th of seven here (1m, firm) on handicap debut, 1l off Atalanta Mist; dam 1m2f/1m4f winner

runners.name: Timely Salute
runners.spotlight: Debut (7f on Chelmsford AW) has seemingly been better than what's followed; only fifth of six on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW; 10-1) last time but there's stamina on the dam's side.
runners.comment: 5th of six on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW; 10-1) last time; stamina on dam's side

runners.name: Echo Valley
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m winner; increasingly big prices when well held in two maidens on AW (1m3f and 9.4f) and a novice on turf at Nottingham (1m, good); has no solid form claims but handicap debut should give her more of a chance.
runners.comment: Has no solid form claims but making her handicap debut should give her more of a chance

runners.name: Baynoona
runners.spotlight: Has an underwhelming strike-rate but her Bath form over 1m features sole win (last September) and a respectable second on latest outing; place claims if proving as effective back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has respectable 1m form at Bath; place claims if transferring it back to 1m2f
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Graffiti
runners.spotlight: On a long losing spell but has shaped encouragingly in two AW outings this year and remains on a workable mark; effective on turf and difficult to dismiss.
runners.comment: On a long losing spell but has shaped encouragingly in two AW races this year

runners.name: Grand Harbour
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden and isn't particularly solid on his form for current yard, comfortably held at Bath (1m) most recently.
runners.comment: Still a maiden; comfortably held at Bath most recently

runners.name: My O My
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden whose recent efforts over further suggest this drop back to 1m2f is well worth exploring; bigger effort is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Maiden who may show some improvement for this drop back in trip

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Record is only 2-52 but he gained the latest success in comfortable fashion at Bath (1m, good) last week and warrants respect under 4lb penalty; recorded the other win over 1m2f (on AW).
runners.comment: Registered a comfortable win at Bath last week; respected under 4lb penalty

runners.name: Blue Hero
runners.spotlight: Nine-time course scorer, most recently last August, but this seasonal debut doesn't look the time to catch him; has never scored when fresh.
runners.comment: Nine-time course scorer but never when fresh; makes seasonal debut

runners.name: Ciotog
