runners.spotlight: Has done all his winning at about 1m, including four times at Bath; has bits of 1m2f form but doesn't look the percentage call; losing run is mounting up.
runners.comment: All wins at about 1m, including four times at Bath; has bits of 1m2f form

runners.name: Galactic Glow
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner who returns here off a workable mark; on the downside, he's been absent for four months and doesn't have a convincing record when fresh.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner but has never scored when fresh; absent for four months

runners.name: Wah May Wai Wai
runners.spotlight: 0-26 in Hong Kong; not far behind a couple of these when fifth over C&D four weeks ago, but will do well to fare any better from the widest stall.
runners.comment: 0-26 in Hong Kong; will do well to figure from the widest stall

runners.name: Dragon Sunrise
runners.spotlight: 0-19; came mighty close to shedding his maiden tag over C&D in March, but has a bit to find with a couple of these on his latest performance; needs to better that effort, albeit Zac Purton keeps the faith.
runners.comment: 0-19 and has a bit to find with a couple of these on his latest performance
runners.owner: Sunny Wong Chi Tat & Wong Tsz Wah

runners.name: Noble Fans
runners.spotlight: 0-14 with his best efforts coming over shorter trips at Sha Tin; was attempting this far for the first time when behind two of these over C&D three weeks ago; more needed and he isn't well drawn.
runners.comment: 0-14; behind two of these over C&D three weeks ago; more needed and he isn't well drawn

runners.name: Setanta
runners.spotlight: Two of his four wins have come over C&D and he was twice placed over this trip during the winter, but recent efforts not so hot and the drop to Class 5 made little difference last time; others stronger.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, but recent efforts not so hot; class drop made no difference last time
runners.owner: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See

runners.name: Zetta Force
runners.spotlight: 0-12, but was only beaten 2l into third in a first-time tongue-tie over C&D four weeks ago (Double Bingo second); would have gone closer with a clearer run; down another 1lb and shortlisted
runners.comment: 0-12, but was only beaten 2l into third over C&D four weeks ago; shortlisted

runners.name: Smiling Emperor
runners.spotlight: 1-21 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D last summer; beaten just over 2l into third behind Podium here three weeks ago and now 7lb better off; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Finished third behind Podium here last time and now 7lb better off; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Windicator Family
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of form over C&D, including a win, and he finished runner-up to a subsequent winner at Sha Tin (1m) last month; his strike-rate of 1-31 in Hong Kong does sound a note of caution, though.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but a strike-rate of 1-31 in Hong Kong does sound a note of caution
runners.owner: Thomas Poon Ho Wing & Poon Chi Keung

runners.name: All Eyes On Me
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made the frame a couple of times over C&D this year, but now 0-19 and has it all to do from 4lb out of the weights if getting a run.
runners.comment: 0-19 and has it all to do from 4lb out of the weights if getting a run
runners.owner: Enrico Chow Yue Hin & Jennifer Ng Mau Yee

runners.name: Telecom Power
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over C&D this year year, but was behind three of these here three weeks ago; better drawn this time, but still hard to recommend given his record of 1-32 in Hong Kong; visor replaces blinkers.
runners.comment: 1-32 in Hong Kong; behind three of these over C&D three weeks ago; visor on
runners.owner: Cheung King Shek, Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al

runners.name: Double Bingo
runners.spotlight: 1-22 in Hong Kong; was attempting beyond 7f for the first time when beaten a short head over C&D four weeks ago; a similar performance would give him every chance.
runners.comment: 1-22 in Hong Kong; beaten a short head over C&D four weeks ago; every chance
runners.owner: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho

runners.name: Prince Alex
runners.spotlight: Two wins over C&D at the end of 2024, but out of the frame in all 12 starts since; the drop in class is a positive, but he still needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins at the end of 2024, but out of the frame in all starts since; down in class

runners.name: Manythanks Forever
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 7f at Sha Tin; still 7lb higher than for the latest and opposable back up in trip from a wide draw.
runners.comment: Both wins have come over 7f at Sha Tin; opposable back up in trip from a wide draw
runners.owner: Chan Wing Kwong, Patrick Ng Pak Hong Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Generations
runners.spotlight: Reserve; both wins have come over 6f here; was attempting this far for the first time when seventh over C&D a week ago and will still need to prove his stamina if getting a run.
runners.comment: Both wins have come over 6f here; will still need to prove his stamina if getting a run

runners.name: Podium
runners.spotlight: All three wins here, the latest over this trip three weeks ago when he had three of these behind; 6lb higher, but crucially stays in Class 5 company and another big effort is likely.
runners.comment: Won over C&D three weeks ago; crucially stays in Class 5; another big effort is likely

runners.name: Awesome Treasure
runners.spotlight: Has regressed in five starts for this yard, but is 1lb lower than when winning over C&D in December and he has the plum draw; can't be dismissed entirely.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than when winning over C&D in December and has the plum draw; can't be dismissed

runners.name: Nebraskan
runners.spotlight: Made it 30th-time lucky in Hong Kong when making all over C&D three weeks ago, but a 6lb rise sends him back into Class 4 level; will need more if he is to follow up.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D three weeks ago, but a 6lb rise sends him back up into Class 4 company
runners.owner: Kenneth Lam Sze Ken, Timothy Junior Lam & T Fok Kwong Hang

runners.name: Forza Leader
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Australia but out of the frame in all nine starts in Hong Kong; well drawn, but still hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all nine starts in Hong Kong; well drawn, but still hard to warm to

runners.name: Voyage Boss
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-10 since his successful Sha Tin debut (6f) a year ago, though not beaten far on several occasions; will appreciate the return to this trip if getting a run.
runners.comment: 0-10 since his winning Sha Tin debut a year ago, albeit not beaten far on a few occasions
runners.owner: Tommy Ching Tsun Wah, Fujisawa Tasayoshi Et Al

runners.name: Healthy Healthy
runners.spotlight: Four wins here, three over this trip, but despite several frame finishes his losing run is up to 21; didn't show enough on his return from four months off here (5f) three weeks ago to make him a compelling candidate; blinkers return.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 21; didn't show enough on his return to make him a compelling candidate
runners.owner: Lo Kui Lun, Lo Hoe Et Al

runners.name: Forerunner
runners.spotlight: 2-31 with both wins coming over 7f at Sha Tin, but he has run well in his last two starts over this C&D since the cheekpieces went back on, only going down by a short head on his penultimate outing; respected from his good draw.
runners.comment: Has run well in his last two starts over C&D since the cheekpieces went back on; respected
runners.owner: Ng Tai Wing, Anson Or Yiu Wah & Henry Yeung Mun Kin

runners.name: King Cannon
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown a great deal in three starts, two of them over C&D, and is drawn wide.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown a great deal in three starts, two of them over C&D, and is drawn wide

runners.name: Marvel And Gold
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in New Zealand but 0-9 in Hong Kong; needs to find plenty more.
runners.comment: 2-2 in New Zealand but 0-9 in Hong Kong; needs to find plenty more

runners.name: Charming Babe
runners.spotlight: Both local wins have come over C&D, the latest in November; held back down at Class 4 level last month, but that was over 7f at Sha Tin; shortlisted from a good draw back here.
runners.comment: Both local wins have come over C&D; shortlisted from a good draw back here

runners.name: World Hero
runners.spotlight: Up another 7lb in his bid for a C&D hat-trick and has the outside stall, but that was also the case for his latest victory; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Up 7lb in his bid for a C&D hat-trick; drawn widest, but that was also the case last time

runners.name: King Oberon
runners.spotlight: Edging back closer to the mark from which he recorded the latest of two C&D victories in the autumn; still needs more, though, and has a wide draw.
runners.comment: Edging back closer to his last winning mark, but still needs more and is drawn wide

runners.name: Blasted Talent
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts over 6f/7f at Sha Tin (one on dirt); finished runner-up in his latest trial, but more will be needed back in a proper race.
runners.comment: Well held in two starts at Sha Tin; runner-up in his latest trial, but more will be needed

runners.name: Beauty Glory
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who is back down to Class 4 level for the first time since successful over 1m here in November; drawn wider than ideal, but still high on list.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; back to Class 4 for the first time since winning over 1m here in November

races.race_name: The French May Trophy Handicap (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Seward
runners.spotlight: 3-8 when trained in Ireland, but only beat one home on his local debut at Sha Tin (7f) in November; hasn't shown much in four trials since March and probably best watched.
runners.comment: Beat one home on local debut at Sha Tin in November; not shown much in four trials since
runners.owner: David Philip Boehm & Anthony Cheung Hin Shun

runners.name: All Round Winner
runners.spotlight: Had two of these behind when winning over C&D four weeks ago; remains unexposed at the trip, but a 6lb rise will demand more of him.
runners.comment: Had two of these behind when winning over C&D four weeks ago; 6lb rise demands more of him
runners.owner: Clement Chan Kam Wing, Maggie Ho Sze Nga Et Al

runners.name: Fortunate Son
runners.spotlight: Two wins over C&D at Class 4 level this season, and has made the frame in two of his four starts since returning to Class 3; has enjoyed better luck with the draw than of late and could go well.
runners.comment: Two wins over C&D at Class 4 level this season; better drawn than of late

runners.name: Pray For Justice
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but out of the frame in two starts at Sha Tin (7f); needs to find more.
runners.comment: 1-1 in Australia, but out of the frame in two starts at Sha Tin (7f); needs to find more

runners.name: Stormi
runners.spotlight: Often makes the running these days; completed a hat-trick over C&D in Class 4 company at the start of the year, but although not disgraced back at Class 3 level (poorly drawn last time) he will need to find more to win in this grade.
runners.comment: Completed a hat-trick over C&D at the start of the year; more still needed in this grade

runners.name: Super Unicorn
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than for the latest of two wins over C&D in the autumn, but will need to better his last two performances.
runners.comment: 1lb higher than for the latest of two wins in the autumn, but below form last two starts

runners.name: Igor Stravinsky
runners.spotlight: Promising in Ireland as a 3yo, but his form in Hong Kong has been patchy; both wins (including one on dirt) have come over 6f and he has something to prove on his first attempt beyond 7f.
runners.comment: Form in Hong Kong has been patchy; something to prove on his first attempt beyond 7f
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Cyrus Li Kin Wai

runners.name: Romantic Gladiator
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in Britain and has progressed in four starts since arriving in Hong Kong, going down by half a length to All Round Winner over C&D four weeks ago; 5lb better off so a good chance of reversing the placings from the inside draw, especially with Zac Purton taking over in the saddle.
runners.comment: Beaten half a length by All Round Winner four weeks ago and 5lb better off; good chance

runners.name: Fivefortwo
runners.spotlight: 2-5 over C&D, the latest win coming in March, but following an 8lb rise he finished behind a couple of these back here four weeks ago; will need to pull out a bit more but he is well drawn.
runners.comment: Behind a couple of these here four weeks ago; needs to pull out a bit more, but well drawn

runners.name: Can't Go Wong
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (1m/1m1f), but a record of 2-50 in Hong Kong shows that he is usually worth taking on.
runners.comment: Dual course winner, but a record of 2-50 in Hong Kong shows that he is worth taking on

runners.name: Romantic Laos
runners.spotlight: Four wins over C&D, but losing run up to 20 and he has 3l to find with Mighty Steed on their meeting here a fortnight ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Losing run now 20; has 3l to find with Mighty Steed on their meeting here a fortnight ago

runners.name: Savvy Warrior
runners.spotlight: 2-9 in Britain but unplaced in all nine local starts, albeit not disgraced in both outings over C&D; frame possibilties.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all nine local starts, albeit not disgraced in both outings over C&D

runners.name: Noble Pursuit
runners.spotlight: Three course wins (two over this trip) and 3lb lower than when beaten a nose over C&D in February, but proving inconsistent and losing run is up to 19; blinkers return for the first time since his debut.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when beaten a nose over C&D in February, but losing run up to 19

runners.name: Sure Joyful
runners.spotlight: Reserve; lost his way after winning over C&D a year ago, but having dropped in the weights he was only beaten a short head and a nose into third here three weeks ago; each-way shout if getting a run.
runners.comment: Only beaten a short head and a nose into third here three weeks ago; each-way shout
runners.owner: Star Chan Iu Seng, Simon Chan Cham Man Et Al

runners.name: Lakeshore Hero
runners.spotlight: On a fair mark on the pick of his form, but is just 1-18 and his best efforts have come over shorter trips.
runners.comment: 1-18 and his best efforts have come over shorter trips

runners.name: Whowrotethatsong
runners.spotlight: Improved on previous efforts over C&D five weeks ago, but still only finished seventh; needs to take another big step forward.
runners.comment: Improved on previous efforts over C&D five weeks ago, but still only finished seventh

runners.name: Ace Power
runners.spotlight: Almost exclusively campaigned at 6f with all three wins coming over that trip here; well held in one previous attempt over this far (on dirt); drawn wide and opposable.
runners.comment: Almost exclusively campaigned at 6f; well held in one previous attempt over this far
runners.owner: Chan Ngan Chi & Chen Yung Hung

runners.name: Dashing Maurison
runners.spotlight: Comfortably made it 11th-time lucky in a Class 5 over 1m1f here a fortnight ago, the switch to blinkers possibly making a difference; will need more in order to follow up back in Class 4 company, though.
runners.comment: Made it 11th-time lucky in a Class 5 here a fortnight ago, but more needed at this level

runners.name: Beauty Gemini
runners.spotlight: Reserve; winner of three barrier trials, but well beaten in three races over 5f/6f at Sha Tin; will need to improve if getting in.
runners.comment: Winner of three trials, but well beaten in three races at Sha Tin; will need to improve
runners.owner: Yuki Kong Yuk Ki & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: General Redwood
runners.spotlight: Improved when narrowly off the mark at the fourth attempt over C&D last month, but he finished behind a couple of these back here a fortnight ago and has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Off the mark over C&D last month, but behind a couple of these back here a fortnight ago

runners.name: Celtic Times
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, the latest from 3lb lower in January; has a wide draw to overcome, though.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, the latest from 3lb lower in January; has a wide draw to overcome

runners.name: Mighty Steed
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who is only 1-22 in Hong Kong but, despite the widest stall, very nearly doubled his tally when beaten a short head over C&D a fortnight ago; major chance.
runners.comment: Very nearly doubled his Hong Kong tally when beaten a short head over C&D a fortnight ago

runners.name: Mega Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who has been running well over 1m at Sha Tin lately (312); has to show he can produce similar form on his first visit here, but a player if he can.
runners.comment: Has been running well at Sha Tin lately; a player if showing similar form here

runners.name: Le Zonda
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; yet to be placed after five Hong Kong starts, but ran well on consecutive outings here (1m1f/1m) in March/April and may not have been ideally suited by soft ground when just below that level at Sha Tin recently; continues to edge down the weights; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 0-5 in Hong Kong but has posted some good efforts here this year and his mark is sliding

runners.name: Noisy Boy
runners.spotlight: Has arrested his decline in recent weeks and looked unlucky not to finish closer than seventh on dirt at Sha Tin seven days ago, but his last win came in January 2025 and he's patently not as good as he was.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have arrested his decline but he's patently not as good as he was
runners.owner: Leung Lun Ping & Ricky Leung Ghin Ming

runners.name: Dragon On Snow
runners.spotlight: Looked promising as a 2yo in Ireland but, following a long absence, launched Hong Kong career with three disappointing runs in March/April (7f-1m).
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with three disappointing runs in March/April (7f-1m)
runners.owner: Eddie Hon Hoi Kwong, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Ace War
runners.spotlight: Started off for Mark Newnham with two emphatic course wins in March (1m3f/1m1f) and again ran well before succumbing to strong late burst from The Auspicious over C&D last month; may still have more to offer for new stable.
runners.comment: Started off for new stable with two emphatic wins and ran well in defeat last time

runners.name: So You Will
runners.spotlight: Didn't fire in C&D contest won by The Auspicious three weeks ago and now 0-6 in Hong Kong, but he ran well over 1m three consecutive times on turf/dirt in February-April and has claims today if first-time blinkers have positive effect.
runners.comment: Didn't fire last time but has claims if new blinkers quickly get him back on track

runners.name: Keefy
runners.spotlight: Consistent 7yo who had an awkward draw when only fifth over 1m here last month and made the frame on his four previous appearances (1m-1m1f); recent 2lb drop helps; likely contender.
runners.comment: Consistent 7yo who has recently dropped 2lb; probably won't be far away

runners.name: The Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Four wins this season, the latest when finishing strongly to collar Ace War over C&D three weeks ago; latest 6lb rise demands even more from him today, though.
runners.comment: Four wins this season, the latest when finishing strongly over C&D last month

runners.name: Liveandletlive
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but has made the frame on nine of his 11 starts and is suited by this C&D; drawn on the inside and has very obvious each-way claims.
runners.comment: Yet to win this season but has made the frame on nine of his 11 starts

runners.name: Flying Luck
runners.spotlight: Suffered worrying slump in form after 1m win at Sha Tin in January, but was seemingly back in good nick when fourth there (1m2f) ten days ago and has possibilities if in the right mood again here.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good nick when fourth at Sha Tin recently; a possible
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs H Ho Yiu Wing, H Garrick Sai Man & H Leslie Sai Kit

runners.name: The Azure
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner last season; not firing on all cylinders this term but has become well handicapped and the booking of Zac Purton catches the eye.
runners.comment: Below best this season but on good mark now and Zac Purton is enlisted today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Ho Pui Lun & Mr & Mrs Norman Tang Fai Ng

runners.name: Harmony Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Led close home to capitalise upon a reduced mark here (1m1f) last month and remains well treated on form as recent as the autumn; also suited by 1m and can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Scored here (1m1f) last month and remains well handicapped on form as recent as the autumn

runners.name: Sturdy Ruby
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 2lb higher in October; has been fighting a losing battle in Class 3 dirt races in recent months but he's of renewed interest now back on turf and back in this grade.
runners.comment: Has found things tough in Class 3 dirt races but is of renewed interest back on turf
runners.owner: Adam Chan Yam Man,Brian Wong Chi Fai & Alfred Chan Yam Lung

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: Third over C&D in February but form has dipped again since, with refitted blinkers (retained) doing little to perk him up last time.
runners.comment: Third over C&D in February but form has dipped again since
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Take Action
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing here last time but came second on three of his four appearances before that and is nicely drawn on the inside; features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Slightly disappointing last time but earlier form suggests he'll be on the premises

runners.name: Incanto Star
runners.spotlight: Placed on consecutive C&D starts in December/January (no headgear) but his subsequent form in blinkers (retained) has been underwhelming; better drawn than last time but still needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Placed twice over C&D this season but recent efforts have been underwhelming

runners.name: Viva Firecracker
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after eight runs but kept on for third over C&D when upped in trip last month and may still have untapped potential over 1m.
runners.comment: 0-8 but kept on for third over C&D when upped in trip last month

runners.name: Run Run Timing
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 5yo who made all in Class 5 C&D contest in February and has finished second twice in this higher grade since; wide draw makes things awkward today, though.
runners.comment: Second twice in this higher grade since Class 5 win in February; drawn wide, though
runners.owner: Tang Ming, Tang Chak Leung Et Al

runners.name: Glorious Ryder
runners.spotlight: Reserve; off the mark at ninth attempt, dead-heating in Class 5 C&D contest in March; subsequent defeat on dirt debut can probably be overlooked but he still has something to prove in this grade.
runners.comment: Dead-heated over C&D in March but has something to prove in this higher grade

runners.name: Precision Hope
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all six starts; not yet exposed over 1m and certainly not devoid of ability but, nevertheless, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Not devoid of ability but yet to be placed after six runs

runners.name: Smiling One
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on all three starts (7f-1m) and seemingly in need of considerable help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on all three starts (7f-1m)

runners.name: Fortune Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; largely consistent 4yo who is now only 2lb higher than when winning over C&D in January; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 4yo who is now only 2lb higher than when winning over C&D in January
runners.owner: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong

runners.name: Gazeley
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning on 2yo debut in Britain but has struggled in Hong Kong (unplaced all six starts).
runners.comment: Looked promising when winning on 2yo debut in Britain but has struggled in Hong Kong
runners.owner: Billie Lam Chi Fan, Lee Shek Tang Et Al

runners.name: Shooting To Top
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who held on by a short head over C&D three weeks ago, his third start; 5lb higher now but has scope for further progress; respected.
runners.comment: Held on for narrow C&D win last month and remains unexposed after only three runs

runners.name: Golden Empire
runners.spotlight: Regressive 8yo; too well handicapped to rule out but managed only midfield finishes from the inside stall here on last two outings and needs today's headgear switch to give him a boost.
runners.comment: Managed only midfield finishes from the inside stall here on last two outings

runners.name: Winning Now
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; made bold bid from the front and lost out only by a short head over C&D a fortnight ago; no surprise if he gives this another good shot from stall 2.
runners.comment: 0-7 but nearly made all over C&D two weeks ago and has a favorable draw today

runners.name: Tactical Command
runners.spotlight: Came second over C&D in February and wasn't beaten far when sixth here last month, but has generally been disappointing this season; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Came second over C&D in February but has generally been disappointing this season

runners.name: Young Arrow
runners.spotlight: Appreciated ease in grade when close third over C&D three weeks ago; remains on a good mark but outside draw an obvious negative today.
runners.comment: Close third when dropped to this grade last month, but wide drawn an obvious negative

runners.name: Jolly Companion
runners.spotlight: Still 5lb higher than when winning by a neck over C&D in January, but recent efforts have been perfectly respectable and he's well drawn today; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have been perfectly respectable and he's well drawn

runners.name: Lucky McQueen
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D in March and has performed well here on both starts since, more recently when close fifth behind Vigor Eye from stall 12 here three weeks ago; has another tough draw to contend with, though.
runners.comment: In prime form but has another tough draw to contend with

runners.name: Jumbo Blessing
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who has shown clear promise on two of his three 5f starts here; looks ready for a crack at 6f and Joao Moreira keeps the ride; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who has shown clear promise in two of his three 5f starts here
runners.owner: Billy Chan Wing Kwong, Kiki Chan Yan Ki & A Chan Fong Ki

runners.name: Leading Agility
runners.spotlight: Close fourth over C&D on both starts for new stable and still lightly raced after only five runs overall; swaps hood for first-time cheekpieces; high on the list.
runners.comment: Close fourth over C&D on both stable starts and still lightly raced after only five runs
runners.owner: Man Pui Kwan & Ma Pak Hung

runners.name: Smart Fighter
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, most recently when making virtually all five weeks ago; combination of 6lb rise and double-digit draw probably enough to leave him vulnerable, though.
runners.comment: Won from the front here last month; probably vulnerable from stall 10 after 6lb rise
runners.owner: The Executors Of The Estate Of The Late Joseph Chung

runners.name: Vigor Eye
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when scoring narrowly over C&D six weeks ago, his sixth start; 5lb higher and not so well drawn today but probably still has more to offer.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner last month, his sixth start; probably still has more to offer

runners.name: Rainbow Seven
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has made the frame on both starts since breakthrough win over C&D in February; respected in current mood.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on both starts since breakthrough win over C&D in February; respected
runners.owner: Jason Chow Hei Shing & Chow Yuk Yin

runners.name: Cornerstone
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on first four Hong Kong starts (all over 6f at Sha Tin); drops in grade here but still needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on first four Hong Kong starts; drops in grade today
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: Amazing Victory
runners.spotlight: Caused 30-1 surprise over C&D at end of last season but returned from layoff with two low-key runs in March/April and is still 4lb above that last winning mark; favourable draw not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Back from layoff with two low-key runs; favourable draw not enough to tempt

runners.name: Tycoon Resources
runners.spotlight: Reserve; led close home to make winning debut over 5f here last month; will need to be taken very seriously if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Led close home to make winning debut over 5f here last month

runners.name: Telecom Fighters
runners.spotlight: Now 7lb lower than when placed over C&D in February, but looked out of sorts on both appearances last month and has a tough draw today.
runners.comment: On good mark now but was in poor form last month and has tough draw today

runners.name: Sunny Da Best
runners.spotlight: Reserve; placed over 7f at Sha Tin two starts ago and kept on for respectable fifth over C&D there last time; another 2lb lower here and will have each-way claims at least if he gets a run.
runners.comment: In fairly good form over 6f/7f lately; holds each-way claims at least

runners.name: Prestige Always
runners.spotlight: Gave pretty good account when sixth over C&D from stall 11 last month and continues to slip down the weights, but he's drawn even wider today.
runners.comment: Ran well when sixth from stall 11 here last month but is drawn even wider today
runners.owner: Eric Yeung Ting Fung & Angel Wong Sau Kuen

runners.name: Flash Current
runners.spotlight: Went very close over 7f at Sha Tin in January, but was not in same form over that C&D last month and will probably need a strong pace to aim at over this trip.
runners.comment: Has gone close over 7f this season; needs a strong pace to aim at over this trip

runners.name: Harmony N Blessed
runners.spotlight: Well below best form this year and not getting any younger, but suggested he might be ready to exploit some help from the handicapper when second over C&D last month; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Long in the tooth now but came second off much-reduced mark here last month
runners.owner: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al

runners.name: Somelovefromabove
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner in Class 4 this season; creditable third here when upped to this grade last month, but drawn wider today and needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins this season; place in this higher grade last time; drawn wider today
runners.owner: Ling Wai Sun & Michael Ling Siu Ming

runners.name: Motor
runners.spotlight: Led close home over C&D in March and was placed off today's mark here last month; moves up in grade today but has the plum inside draw and is evidently in good nick; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Moves up in grade today but is in good form and has the plum inside draw

runners.name: Red Elegance
runners.spotlight: Reserve; regressive 6yo who is yet to be placed after nine starts this season and is better suited by 5f.
runners.comment: Regressive 6yo who is yet to be placed this season and is better suited by 5f

runners.name: Amazing Kid
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who has collected four C&D wins this season; didn't run badly when midfield from stall 12 here last month, but is drawn wide again and needs to kick on.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins this season; needs to kick on again to overcome wide stall

runners.name: Matters Most
runners.spotlight: Gave it a good shot from the front when second over C&D last month, but is not so well drawn today and his record this season is very mixed; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Second over C&D last month but not so well drawn today and this season's record is mixed

runners.name: Sovereign Fund
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with C&D win on stable debut in February and put two lesser efforts behind him when placed here last month, but has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark over C&D three weeks ago, but has become hard to predict
runners.owner: Glenn Chan Wai Cheung & Raymond Chan Ka Shun

runners.name: Son Pak Fu
runners.spotlight: Seven-time C&D winner; on the downgrade now and did not get competitive over 5f here a fortnight ago, but combination of drop in grade, favourable draw and return to 6f makes him of interest today.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but drops in grade and has favourable draw today; one to consider

runners.name: Aurio
runners.spotlight: Progressive for new stable this season, winning twice (once over C&D) and making the frame on his other five appearances; well drawn today and ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Progressive for new stable this season (two wins) and likely to be on the premises again

runners.name: Dragon Four Seas
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well over C&D on seasonal debut in September but his form nosedived afterwards; needs to have been revitalised by combination of four-month break and switch to new stable.
runners.comment: Badly out of form when last in action; makes stable debut after a break
runners.owner: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Moments In Time
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in Group 3 handicap at Sha Tin (1m1f) in November but has lost his way since; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Creditable third in Group 3 handicap at Sha Tin in November but has lost his way since

runners.name: Lo Rider
runners.spotlight: Will be suited by today's move back up in trip, but has basically been a bit disappointing since winning over C&D on seasonal debut in September and is still 2lb above that mark; sports refitted hood and first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Yet to build upon C&D win on seasonal debut; new headgear combination tried

runners.name: Kaholo Angel
runners.spotlight: Now back on same mark as when dead-heating with Helene Feeling over C&D in December, his first attempt at 1m, but his form since the turn of the year has been uninspiring.
runners.comment: Dead-heated off today's mark over C&D in December but well below that form this year
runners.owner: Ho Garrick Sai Man & Tam Wing Yee

runners.name: Packing Angel
runners.spotlight: Close third in last season's Hong Kong Derby; yet to hit top gear this term but kept on for third at Sha Tin ten days ago and has slipped to a good mark.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Sha Tin ten days ago and has slipped to a good mark

runners.name: Silvery Breeze
runners.spotlight: Pipped a next-time-out winner here last month and has now won twice over C&D this season; might still be on the upgrade; good chance.
runners.comment: Beat a next-time-out winner over C&D last month and might still be on the upgrade

runners.name: Beauty Alliance
runners.spotlight: Placed twice here in March before showing career-best form to win by a neck over C&D last month; up in grade and drawn wide today but ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month; up in grade and drawn wide but ought to give another good account

runners.name: Infinite Resolve
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when second in Classic Mile at Sha Tin in February, but his recent 7f handicap form there has been underwhelming and he comes here with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Second in Classic Mile in February but recent handicap form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Awesome Fluke
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner this season (1m1f/1m); looked unlucky not to finish closer than fourth at Sha Tin (1m; denied clear run) two starts ago and only faded out of contention in closing stages when fifth there (1m1f) last time; ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Dual course winner this season; still in good form; ought to be on the premises again

runners.name: Helene Feeling
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two course wins (1m1f/1m) and was placed behind Speed Dragon in Group 3 handicap here (1m1f) in January; not quite at same level since but his recent fourth at Sha Tin was a fairly good effort and he's dropped 2lb since; a possible.
runners.comment: Not quite at best lately but has dropped another 2lb since recent Sha Tin fourth

runners.name: Speed Dragon
runners.spotlight: Took form to new level when cosily winning Group 3 handicap here (1m1f) in January and has been placed off today's mark on both starts since (1m1f/7f); likely contender again.
runners.comment: Placed twice off today's mark since winning Group 3 handicap here (1m1f) in January

runners.name: Soleil Fighter
runners.spotlight: Posted career-best performance when making all to score over 1m1f here in February, but was safely held off new mark at Sha Tin the following month and needs to find hidden reserves today.
runners.comment: Career-best form when winning here in February; safely held off new mark since

runners.name: Nikkaluokta
runners.owner: Gerald Laroche, Ecurie Bertrand Milliere Et Al

runners.name: Djin's
runners.owner: Sacha Tubiana, Chauvigny Global Equine Sasu Et Al

races.race_name: Sky Bet Race To The Ebor Jorvik Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Fireblade
runners.spotlight: Did well last season, including a win over 1m4f in April but he was best later on at about 2m (including at York); reappearance this term was pretty encouraging, considering it was over just 1m2f, and he looks a serious each-way contender.
runners.comment: Reappearance this term was encouraging; looks a serious each-way contender
runners.owner: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks

runners.name: Klassleader
runners.spotlight: Made his debut only last June and has had just four races; long odds-on for 1m4f novice win in August and best form when second of six to Sing Us A Song on handicap debut at Haydock (1m6f, again good to soft; came from the back) in September; probably still a work in progress at that stage and he starts 2026 (for in-form yard) with significant potential.
runners.comment: Only four starts; best form when 2nd on handicap debut in September; potential still there

runners.name: Plage De Havre
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year off 11lb lower; competitive almost every time last season, ending on a major high with 4l win at Haydock (1m4f, good) in July; however, there have been a couple of blips and last month's poor show in Listed race at Kempton was one of those; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Third last year off 11lb lower; poor show at Kempton last month and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Sing Us A Song
runners.spotlight: Disappointed over 1m4f at Royal Ascot and Glorious Goodwood last summer but returned from a gelding operation to win at Haydock (sole run at 1m6f, good to soft) in September; made a satisfactory reappearance at Epsom 15 days ago and is still lightly raced, so could have more to offer; good ground and softer would probably be better for him than good to firm.
runners.comment: Made a satisfactory reappearance at Epsom 15 days ago; could have more to offer

runners.name: Dark Moon Rising
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2023; no joy anywhere last season (mostly at this track) but the closest he came was a neck defeat here (1m2f; 4lb higher today) on final start; not far away on AW last three starts this year and the same may be possible on today's return to turf.
runners.comment: Not far away on AW last three starts this year; the same may be possible on turf return

runners.name: Stressfree
runners.spotlight: Beaten a head in this race last year and fourth of 22 here in the Ebor in August; began 2026 with another solid effort, when second at Ripon; not a prolific winner with his waiting tactics but big each-way chance.
runners.comment: Beaten a head in this race last year; not a prolific winner but big each-way chance

runners.name: Prince Of The Seas
runners.spotlight: Two good runs last August included a narrow win at Goodwood (1m4f, good to firm) but he was soundly beaten on starts either side of those; sold out of Ralph Beckett's stable for 30,000gns in October and not disgraced on reappearance at Ripon 18 days ago; tongue tied last four starts and now also tries cheekpieces; others are a bit more persuasive.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on reappearance at Ripon 18 days ago; others are a bit more persuasive

runners.name: King's Code
runners.spotlight: Second at this meeting in 2024 but only eighth in this race last year; had an excellent autumn on AW and won again in January, but he's struggled to make an impact in recent AW starts and his record is a lot stronger at up to 1m3f than at about 1m4f.
runners.comment: Struggled to make impact in recent AW starts; record much stronger at up to 1m3f than 1m4f

runners.name: Will Scarlet
runners.spotlight: Four French wins for Andre Fabre and thereabouts for new trainers in four consecutive races in Dubai this January-March, winning by 4l from the front in a 1m4f handicap on final occasion; first equipped with cheekpieces that day (they are retained) and it was a tactical switch too; could have more to give with that combination but his handicap mark has been raised significantly.
runners.comment: Ex-French; won latest Meydan handicap (1m4f) for new yard by 4l, from front in cheekpieces

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Four wins (22 races) at up to 1m2f on AW/good to soft when trained in France, before 100,000euros sale last July; finishing towards rear when an outsider in warm races at Kempton (1m3f, AW; hooded) and Musselburgh (1m1f; usual tongue-tie back on) was not a great start to his British career but he's been dropped 3lb after both of those races.
runners.comment: Four wins in France but not a great start to British career; dropped 3lb both British runs
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: Paddy The Squire
runners.spotlight: Short-headed on 2024 reappearance and won it last year; thrived last season when winning three races and not far away in the other three, including at this track; highly respected for that campaign, and first-time-out record, but the handicapper reacted and he now needs to resume improvement.
runners.comment: Handicapper reacted to last season's excellent campaign; needs to resume improvement

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over 5f at Wolverhampton since joining this yard, with the most recent success coming off 3lb lower; solid effort at Southwell (6f) 13 days ago but now steps up in grade on this switch back to turf.
runners.comment: Has won twice over 5f at Wolverhampton since joining this yard; now steps up in grade

runners.name: Kinswoman
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; progressive last year, winning on three occasions, including when making all at Haydock (5f, good) when last seen in October; now 5lb higher but this well-bred filly looks the type to continue progressing as a 4yo; holds an entry in Group 1 King Charles III at Royal Ascot.
runners.comment: Progressive as 3yo; 5lb higher than for Haydock win; holds Group 1 entry at Royal Ascot

runners.name: Michaela's Boy
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; won at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January on second start since returning to this stable and he went close over that same C&D next time; ran creditably back on turf at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago but he has a better strike-rate on the AW (1-24 on turf).
runners.comment: Ran creditably at Nottingham last time but his strike-rate on turf now reads 1-24

runners.name: Almaty Star
runners.spotlight: Had been running well on the AW this winter and wasn't for catching after setting a strong pace over this C&D last month (third has won since); an 8lb rise still leaves him on a fair mark based on his previous form but he does now step back up in grade.
runners.comment: Wasn't for catching after setting a strong pace over C&D last time; up 8lb and in grade

runners.name: Naana's Sparkle
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won at Redcar (5f, good to firm) and Goodwood (5f, heavy) last summer, but he was around 5l behind Kinswoman at Haydock (5f, good; first-time tongue-tie) in October on final start for Alice Haynes; watch the betting on debut for new trainer.
runners.comment: Won twice last year but was behind Kinswoman at Haydock in October; new trainer

runners.name: Grandlad
runners.spotlight: Can front-run, as when making all on his reappearance at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago; a 3lb rise for that success looks fair but he's likely to face competition for the lead here.
runners.comment: Made all at Nottingham on his reappearance; likely to face competition for the lead

runners.name: Twilight Fun
runners.spotlight: Front-runner whose last four wins have been on the AW, the most recent over 6f at Newcastle in February, but he did win here (6f) in 2024; failed to beat a rival home having unseated his rider on the way to the start at Newcastle in March and now drops back to 5f on first start since; looks likely to face competition for the lead.
runners.comment: Last four wins have been on the AW; failed to beat a rival home at Newcastle in March

runners.name: Existent
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run at Southwell in January and posted a respectable effort at Newmarket (5f, good) last month; however, he was down the field at Ascot (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago and is now just 1-46 on turf; others preferred.
runners.comment: Ended a long losing run at Southwell in January; well beaten at Ascot and now 1-46 on turf

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Four wins from 40 starts, with the two wins on turf coming at Windsor (good to firm) in 2025; returned from a 65-day break with a good run at Nottingham (5f, good) 17 days ago, beaten a head by Grandlad; contender.
runners.comment: Both turf wins at Windsor but narrowly beaten by Grandlad at Nottingham last time

runners.name: Cressida Wildes
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last July but she's been well beaten in two starts since landing a handicap at Wolverhampton in February, most recently at Ascot four days ago; bit to prove.
runners.comment: C&D winner but been well beaten in two starts since winning at Wolverhampton in February

runners.name: Mesaafi
runners.spotlight: All three wins over 6f on the AW but he does act on turf; had a wide trip on his return from a break but stepped up on that when fourth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; each-way chance.
runners.comment: All three wins have been on the AW over 6f but was a solid fourth at Ascot (5f) recently

runners.name: Forager
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; progressive last year, winning three times, including over this C&D last May before following up at York (5f, good to firm); below best at Lingfield in November though and she's unlikely to get things her own way in front on her first start since.
runners.comment: Progressed last year; below best in November; unlikely to get things her own way in front

runners.name: Rocking Ends
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on good to firm last year, including over C&D in August off a mark 1lb higher than today's; 0-4 in Bahrain this winter but now returns to Britain on a handy mark having been off for 83 days; could have this run to suit and holds claims.
runners.comment: 0-4 in Bahrain this winter but now 1lb lower than for last year's C&D success; has claims

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who, having been gelded, won two in a row over 6f on AW earlier this year; further progress cannot be ruled out but he's finished seventh on his last two starts and needs something extra today.
runners.comment: Kicked off 2026 with 2 wins in a row but seventh on last two starts; needs something extra

runners.name: Fahrenheit Seven
runners.spotlight: Two wins on turf last season for Mark Usher (5f/6f) and he's continued to improve for his new yard, winning two in a row on AW earlier this year (5f); not at his best when seventh of 15 back on grass in a recent Listed race at Naas (5f, good) but shaped as though this return to 6f could be a positive; returns to a handicap and remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW this year; beaten back on turf latest but still one to be interested in

runners.name: American Style
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ripon (6f, good) on last year's reappearance and ran well in defeat on next four starts (including over C&D) prior to two lesser runs to finish his campaign; returned to form with very close third of 16 in same Ripon race last month and this front-runner is an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Very close 3rd of 16 at Ripon on reappearance & this front-runner is an each-way contender

runners.name: Binhareer
runners.spotlight: Promising 3yo last season and things clicked in a major way with a dominant win in a big-field C&D handicap (good) last October when last seen; he's entered in the Group 1 Jubilee Stakes at Royal Ascot and this lightly raced sort is in excellent hands to continue to progress; strong contender.
runners.comment: Dominant in big-field C&D h'cap last October & in excellent hands to continue to progress

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: 6f German Listed win for Archie Watson last June and looked as good as ever when 1l third of 13 to Strike Red in recent Leicester handicap (good to firm), when back up to 6f on his third run for this yard; may not be far away.
runners.comment: Close third of 13 at Leicester last month when back up to 6f and he may not be far away
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Pilgrim
runners.spotlight: Mixed fortunes since winning the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot in 2024 (good to firm); got back on track to win at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month despite being denied a clear run; his peak performances have come at 5f but he was a C&D winner in his 2yo season; respected up just 3lb for last time.
runners.comment: Got back on track to win at Musselburgh last month and he's respected up just 3lb

runners.name: Wicket Keeper
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won maiden/novice races on turf last May/June and was a promising fourth of 14 at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March on his stable debut; however, not quite at the same level there (6f) since and he's up in grade this afternoon; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; possesses some likeable form but he's up in grade & others appeal more

runners.name: Tuco Salamanca
runners.spotlight: Five wins last year (AW/soft) and he improved again to win in good style on his reappearance at Kempton (6f, AW) in March; he's 5-9 on AW as opposed to 1-7 on turf but there can be optimism that he is just as good on grass (has shown promise on various ground) and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Improved again to win in good style at Kempton in March and he's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Pocklington
runners.spotlight: Produced an uncharacteristic below-par run at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month but he's had wind surgery since and was progressive on AW in the winter; twice finished close-up in big-field handicaps over this C&D (good/good to firm) last summer and there's every chance he will be bang there.
runners.comment: Wind op after below-par latest run; progressive previously and has strong C&D form

runners.name: We Never Stop
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf winner who ran well when third at Newcastle (6f, AW) in March; however, the AW seems preferable nowadays and he finished last at Newmarket (5f, good) last month when back on grass.
runners.comment: Ran well on Newcastle AW two starts ago but he seems inferior on turf nowadays

runners.name: Rousing Encore
runners.spotlight: Merely fifth on his last two starts, following a very promising second on his reappearance at Newcastle (6f, AW); however, he won this last year (good to firm) and is handicapped to be in the mix if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Merely 5th on last two runs but won this last year and is on a competitive mark if on song

runners.name: Dark Cloud Rising
runners.spotlight: Three handicap wins during last season's 3yo campaign (6f/5f, including on good/good to firm) and the return of cheekpieces may be a plus; however, he's been unplaced on both runs this year; has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Three wins during last season's 3yo campaign but unplaced on both runs this year

runners.name: Sondad
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last May/June (6f, soft/good to firm) when this visor first went on and he ran well to be placed on his final two outings last autumn; each-way claims if fully tuned up for this reappearance outing.
runners.comment: Did well last season after this visor was applied; each-way claims if fully tuned up

runners.name: Russet Gold
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 19 starts going back to June 2023 but, following a wind op, he was an encouraging fifth off his reduced mark behind Strike Red on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Leicester (6f, good to firm), where he was short of room late on; could build on that with a big run today.
runners.comment: Encouraging 5th on recent stable debut at Leicester, where short of room; possible player

runners.name: Strike Red
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with close third at Newcastle (6f, AW) last month then snapped a losing run when winning at Leicester (6f, good to firm); there's still mileage in his mark judged on old form (including two C&D wins); chance.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester latest; this 2-time C&D winner still has mileage in his mark on old form

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far at Newmarket (6f, good) and Newbury (6f, good to firm) last May and he's well handicapped on that form; however, below best on final two starts last season and likewise on recent reappearance at Newmarket.
runners.comment: On a handy mark but there was no return to form on his recent reappearance at Newmarket

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: Two US Listed wins in 2024 and he twice went close on the St Moritz ice this February; having been detached he kept on for sixth of 11 on his recent stable debut at Goodwood (5f, good); may not be a forlorn hope back up to 6f.
runners.comment: Detached before keeping on over 5f at Goodwood; might not be a forlorn hope back up to 6f

runners.name: Sir Yoshi
runners.spotlight: Ran well in defeat in two 5f Listed races here when trained by David Marnane but well beaten on stable debut over this C&D (good) last October, and a career-best is needed in first-time cheekpieces on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Has some good course form but a career-best is needed on this comeback; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Desert Falcon
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who performed with great credit on all five starts last season, finishing second at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) on his reappearance and beaten just a neck in the Ayr Gold Cup (6f, soft) when last seen; has had wind surgery since; no surprise if he makes a bold bid on this return.
runners.comment: Consistent front-runner who went close in Ayr Gold Cup when last seen; has gone well fresh

runners.name: Tropical Storm
runners.spotlight: His best form is at 5f and he hasn't been at the top of his game since a Listed win at this meeting last May; however, he kept on well for third at Goodwood (5f, good) recently, suggesting the return to 6f is worth exploring, and his two career wins have both come here; a visor goes on.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Goodwood last time and both career wins have come here

runners.name: Woven
runners.spotlight: 10yo who may prefer slow ground but the going was good when he was fourth of 21 in this in 2024; often runs well here and he reappeared with a creditable fourth of 19 at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) in March; each-way possible.
runners.comment: 10yo; 4th in this in 2024; creditable 4th at Doncaster on reappearance; each-way possible
runners.owner: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd

runners.name: Dark Thirty
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten this spring on first two starts for new yard; however, he's 1lb lower than when second of 16 in this race last year for Richard Hannon, and he won here in 2023; capable of bouncing back with a bold show.
runners.comment: Yet to shine for new yard but he's 1lb lower than when second of 16 in this last year

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who held on by a neck in last year's Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) and had excuses for her subsequent defeats in 2025; not disgraced, despite failing to justify favouritism, in Newmarket Group 3 on reappearance; again respected at the weights.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly who won last year's Commonwealth Cup; respected at the weights
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Elmonjed
runners.spotlight: Steps up further in class but he has progressive York form and brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D (gained last win cosily in good-ground Listed race) and 4-9 in headgear, which is reapplied with Newmarket reappearance under his belt; has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D and 4-9 in headgear; one to consider

runners.name: American Affair
runners.spotlight: Has built up a good strike rate over 5f/5.5f and registered a neck success at Royal Ascot (good to firm) upped to Group 1 level when last seen; landed a handicap at this meeting 12 months ago; very progressive and has possibilities provided he's as effective back at 6f.
runners.comment: Very progressive, particularly at 5f/5.5f; Group 1 success at Royal Ascot when last seen

runners.name: Cool Hoof Luke
runners.spotlight: Missed last season; retains every ounce of ability judged on AW performances this year, last time landing a Listed prize at Wolverhampton (7f) to take his overall record to 3-6; possibly still has further improvement in him; interesting returned to the C&D of his 2024 Gimcrack win.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 features wins in the Gimcrack over C&D in 2024 and AW Listed race last time

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive over 6f, mostly in handicaps prior to showing bigger things this season by bagging Listed honours at Doncaster (good to soft) and finishing a good second in Group 3 at Newmarket (good); at the top of his form and may rate higher still; in the mix.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; good second in Newmarket Group 3 most recently; in the mix

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has a strike-rate of 12-34 overall and 3-4 at York, the most recent course success in 7f Group 3 last June (equally effective at 6f); ties in with some of today's opponents on penultimate effort; removal of usual hood backfired last time; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-4 at York, the most recent success in Group 3 last June

runners.name: Regional
runners.spotlight: Still capable of classy form, having been placed in the last two runnings of the 5f Group 1 at Royal Ascot; however, he's on a losing run, being 0-8 since landing the Sprint Cup at Haydock in 2023; getting on and returns to action as an 8yo.
runners.comment: Still capable of classy form but he's 0-8 since landing the 2023 Sprint Cup

runners.name: Big Mojo
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good ground or firmer; drops back in grade for this seasonal debut and even under a 5lb penalty holds a major chance on best Group 1 form last term, namely close second in the July Cup and success in the Sprint Cup (beat Kind Of Blue by just over 1l; both well drawn).
runners.comment: Second in the July Cup and won the Sprint Cup; major contender even with a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Kind Of Blue
runners.spotlight: Very smart performer whose Group 1 record features a win on Champions Day at Ascot (6f, soft) in 2024 and second-place finishes in the last two runnings of the Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good); leading player at the weights, assuming he's in top form (disappointing back at Ascot when last seen).
runners.comment: Group 1 form features a win at Ascot in 2024 and two second-place finishes in Sprint Cup

runners.name: Crestofdistinction
runners.spotlight: Produced peak performance over C&D, namely close second to Elmonjed in Listed event; however, faces a stiffer assignment in this higher grade and his reappearance effort (heavy defeat in Newmarket Group 3) hardly bodes well.
runners.comment: Produced peak performance over C&D but faces a stiffer task in this field

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Listed sprint double at Lingfield this year; has lots of good form on turf, including very close third in this race two years ago and respectable fourth in Newmarket Group 3 last time; still a maiden (0-19) in this sphere, however.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate on AW; has lots of good turf form but is 0-19 in this sphere

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Ran well in last year's Nunthorpe in sole York attempt, finishing 100-1 second, but that's very much an outlier in his form; still needs to prove he can repeat it; failed to beat a rival in Newmarket Group 3 contest on reappearance.
runners.comment: Still needs to repeat the form of his 100-1 second in the 2025 Nunthorpe

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced in last three appearances (various grades) but finished behind some of today's rivals in those contests and needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing sequence that goes back to Group 3 honours two years ago.
runners.comment: Needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing run that goes back two years

runners.name: Moments Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Worth a second look given she's the sole Ballydoyle runner and it's possible she'll improve for the step up in trip on her second start of the season; however, she's been beaten in four Listed/Group races since winning a maiden at Leopardstown on her debut last June, and was 2l behind Legacy Link when fifth in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket last October.
runners.comment: Sole Ballydoyle runner so worth a second look but 0-4 since debut win & improvement needed
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sea The Storm
runners.spotlight: Trained as 2yo by David Menuisier (same owners); won on debut at Sandown (1m, soft) last September and runner-up in German Group 3 (1m, soft) in October; narrowly defied penalty in a novice at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) on her recent stable/seasonal debut; she's unexposed and may well have more to offer but has to take a big step forward from that reappearance win.
runners.comment: Runner-up in German Group 3, with maiden/novice wins either side; needs a step forward

runners.name: Legacy Link
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Haydock (1m, good) last September on her second start and rallied for fourth of ten in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (good) in October, the form of which has worked out very well; her dam (a sister to Frankel) was a 1m4f winner and this step up in trip should be right up her street; leading claims for top yard that has won this seven times since 2011.
runners.comment: Fourth in Group 1 Fillies' Mile; form has worked out well and the step up in trip can suit

runners.name: Felicitas
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and again had something to spare when making it 2-2 in a novice at Sandown (1m2f, good) last month; she goes up in grade today but this exciting prospect appeals as a type who will keep on getting better and better; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 2-2, winning those novices with something to spare; up in grade but an exciting prospect

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Her four juvenile starts yielded one win (1m AW maiden on third outing) and she was a clear second of four in a Class 4 handicap at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) on last month's reappearance; she can win more races but dramatic improvement is necessary if she's to take this.
runners.comment: Clear second in AW handicap on reappearance but dramatic improvement is necessary today

runners.name: K Sarra
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for comfortable win at Southwell (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start, then finished down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) last month on her reappearance; however, the drop back in trip looked very much against her and this sister to Pride Of Arras, who won last season's Dante over C&D for connections, could be an entirely different proposition over today's longer trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Down the field in Fred Darling; could be entirely different proposition at the longer trip

runners.name: Inishbeg
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in Thirsk maiden (7f, good) last time, opening his account at the fourth attempt; may well build on that success and show further improvement, in keeping with his pedigree (siblings include a very useful performer for his connections); respected.
runners.comment: Prevailed in Thirsk maiden last time; may well build on that success and progress further
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: The Resdev Scholar
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo season with a hat-trick; consistent efforts this term, good third at Beverley (7.5f) most recently; getting no respite from the assessor but is reasonably solid and likely to give his running.
runners.comment: Ended his 2yo season with a hat-trick; consistent efforts this term; solid chance
runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Startled
runners.spotlight: Got up in the final strides upped to 7f at the Newmarket Craven meeting (good) last time, taking record in turf handicaps to 2-2; could well improve further and he represents last year's winning yard; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Newmarket win (upped to 7f) took his record in turf handicaps to 2-2; shortlisted

runners.name: Cotai Lights
runners.spotlight: 6f AW scorer who has made the frame over 7f in both turf attempts, including at Newmarket last time when fitted with cheekpieces (retained); however, likely needs some improvement to double his tally.
runners.comment: 6f AW scorer who has made the frame over 7f in both turf attempts, including last time

runners.name: Al Najashi
runners.spotlight: Record of 5-5, all over 6f/7f on AW this year, since handicapping; registered the latest success on finals day at Newcastle; major contender, assuming his tremendous progress can be sustained back on turf.
runners.comment: Major contender, provided his tremendous AW progress can be sustained back on turf

runners.name: Hasbro Market
runners.spotlight: Doesn't seem totally straightforward but he has a solid record (2122) and may do better still; gelded prior to good effort behind Passing Thought at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) on handicap/seasonal debut; in the mix.
runners.comment: Has a solid record and may do better still; good second to Passing Thought on reappearance

runners.name: Montague Menace
runners.spotlight: Possibly didn't take to Epsom (8.5f) last time when beaten favourite on handicap debut; recorded a ready success in Lingfield maiden (7f, AW) in his only other start this term and may still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Possibly didn't take to Epsom last time on handicap debut; may still have more to offer

runners.name: Luansobe
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who most recently belied his 40-1 odds with success at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on seasonal debut; needs to prove he can back up that form and also needs to step up sharply on his sole turf effort.
runners.comment: Belied his 40-1 odds with AW reappearance win; needs to prove he can back up that form

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante who seems to face a difficult task and has something to prove off a mark that stems from a 7lb rise for her apparent good effort in the Fred Darling; failed to back up that form last time.
runners.comment: Failed to back up her Fred Darling effort last time; something to prove off her mark

runners.name: Advance Twentyfive
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably in valuable contest over C&D last autumn but remains a maiden and has a bit to prove off his mark in this warm handicap; failed to show his form at Thirsk (behind Inishbeg) on reappearance.
runners.comment: Below form at Thirsk (behind Inishbeg) on reappearance and remains a maiden

runners.name: Awraad
runners.spotlight: Ran well over 6f in nursery at the York Ebor festival and Listed event at Ripon last August; nowhere near that form in two outings since and has enough to prove off current mark back at 7f.
runners.comment: Good efforts over 6f last August; nowhere near that form in two outings since

runners.name: Gold Queen Kindly
runners.spotlight: Became a tad exposed for Karl Burke and finished behind a couple of today's rivals, admittedly not disgraced, at Wetherby (7f) most recently; subsequently sold for 28,000gns; 0-7 since debut win.
runners.comment: Became a tad exposed for Karl Burke; 0-7 since debut win; first start for new yard

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well in valuable event over C&D last autumn but needs to prove he can transfer that form to handicap level; failed to do so at Pontefract on reappearance, albeit perhaps needed the run.
runners.comment: Needs to prove the form of his autumn C&D effort can be transferred to handicap level

runners.name: First Legion
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in 6f novice event at this meeting last year; has become quite exposed kept to sprinting since but this combination of new trip and return to the Dante festival may have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Combination of new trip and return to the Dante festival may have positive effect

runners.name: First Time
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign for Harry Charlton by winning a Newmarket maiden (6f) and running well in Newcastle handicap (7f, AW); sold for 90,000gns and gelded since; likely needs further progress on stable debut.
runners.comment: Ran well in final 2yo start for Harry Charlton but likely needs further progress

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly who held on by a neck in last year's Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) and had excuses for her subsequent defeats in 2025; not disgraced, despite failing to justify favouritism, in Newmarket Group 3 on reappearance; again respected at the weights.
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly who won last year's Commonwealth Cup; respected at the weights
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Elmonjed
runners.spotlight: Steps up further in class but he has progressive York form and brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D (gained last win cosily in good-ground Listed race) and 4-9 in headgear, which is reapplied with Newmarket reappearance under his belt; has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Brings impressive stats, being 3-4 over C&D and 4-9 in headgear; one to consider

runners.name: American Affair
runners.spotlight: Has built up a good strike rate over 5f/5.5f and registered a neck success at Royal Ascot (good to firm) upped to Group 1 level when last seen; landed a handicap at this meeting 12 months ago; very progressive and has possibilities provided he's as effective back at 6f.
runners.comment: Very progressive, particularly at 5f/5.5f; Group 1 success at Royal Ascot when last seen

runners.name: Cool Hoof Luke
runners.spotlight: Missed last season; retains every ounce of ability judged on AW performances this year, last time landing a Listed prize at Wolverhampton (7f) to take his overall record to 3-6; possibly still has further improvement in him; interesting returned to the C&D of his 2024 Gimcrack win.
runners.comment: Record of 3-6 features wins in the Gimcrack over C&D in 2024 and AW Listed race last time

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive over 6f, mostly in handicaps prior to showing bigger things this season by bagging Listed honours at Doncaster (good to soft) and finishing a good second in Group 3 at Newmarket (good); at the top of his form and may rate higher still; in the mix.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive; good second in Newmarket Group 3 most recently; in the mix

runners.name: Quinault
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has a strike-rate of 12-34 overall and 3-4 at York, the most recent course success in 7f Group 3 last June (equally effective at 6f); ties in with some of today's opponents on penultimate effort; removal of usual hood backfired last time; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Front-runner who is 3-4 at York, the most recent success in Group 3 last June

runners.name: Regional
runners.spotlight: Still capable of classy form, having been placed in the last two runnings of the 5f Group 1 at Royal Ascot; however, he's on a losing run, being 0-8 since landing the Sprint Cup at Haydock in 2023; getting on and returns to action as an 8yo.
runners.comment: Still capable of classy form but he's 0-8 since landing the 2023 Sprint Cup

runners.name: Big Mojo
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good ground or firmer; drops back in grade for this seasonal debut and even under a 5lb penalty holds a major chance on best Group 1 form last term, namely close second in the July Cup and success in the Sprint Cup (beat Kind Of Blue by just over 1l; both well drawn).
runners.comment: Second in the July Cup and won the Sprint Cup; major contender even with a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Kind Of Blue
runners.spotlight: Very smart performer whose Group 1 record features a win on Champions Day at Ascot (6f, soft) in 2024 and second-place finishes in the last two runnings of the Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good); leading player at the weights, assuming he's in top form (disappointing back at Ascot when last seen).
runners.comment: Group 1 form features a win at Ascot in 2024 and two second-place finishes in Sprint Cup

runners.name: Crestofdistinction
runners.spotlight: Produced peak performance over C&D, namely close second to Elmonjed in Listed event; however, faces a stiffer assignment in this higher grade and his reappearance effort (heavy defeat in Newmarket Group 3) hardly bodes well.
runners.comment: Produced peak performance over C&D but faces a stiffer task in this field

runners.name: Diligent Harry
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate on AW includes a Listed sprint double at Lingfield this year; has lots of good form on turf, including very close third in this race two years ago and respectable fourth in Newmarket Group 3 last time; still a maiden (0-19) in this sphere, however.
runners.comment: Good strike-rate on AW; has lots of good turf form but is 0-19 in this sphere

runners.name: Ain't Nobody
runners.spotlight: Ran well in last year's Nunthorpe in sole York attempt, finishing 100-1 second, but that's very much an outlier in his form; still needs to prove he can repeat it; failed to beat a rival in Newmarket Group 3 contest on reappearance.
runners.comment: Still needs to repeat the form of his 100-1 second in the 2025 Nunthorpe

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Far from disgraced in last three appearances (various grades) but finished behind some of today's rivals in those contests and needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing sequence that goes back to Group 3 honours two years ago.
runners.comment: Needs to raise his game somewhat; on a ten-race losing run that goes back two years

runners.name: Moments Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Worth a second look given she's the sole Ballydoyle runner and it's possible she'll improve for the step up in trip on her second start of the season; however, she's been beaten in four Listed/Group races since winning a maiden at Leopardstown on her debut last June, and was 2l behind Legacy Link when fifth in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket last October.
runners.comment: Sole Ballydoyle runner so worth a second look but 0-4 since debut win & improvement needed
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Sea The Storm
runners.spotlight: Trained as 2yo by David Menuisier (same owners); won on debut at Sandown (1m, soft) last September and runner-up in German Group 3 (1m, soft) in October; narrowly defied penalty in a novice at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) on her recent stable/seasonal debut; she's unexposed and may well have more to offer but has to take a big step forward from that reappearance win.
runners.comment: Runner-up in German Group 3, with maiden/novice wins either side; needs a step forward

runners.name: Legacy Link
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Haydock (1m, good) last September on her second start and rallied for fourth of ten in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (good) in October, the form of which has worked out very well; her dam (a sister to Frankel) was a 1m4f winner and this step up in trip should be right up her street; leading claims for top yard that has won this seven times since 2011.
runners.comment: Fourth in Group 1 Fillies' Mile; form has worked out well and the step up in trip can suit

runners.name: Felicitas
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and again had something to spare when making it 2-2 in a novice at Sandown (1m2f, good) last month; she goes up in grade today but this exciting prospect appeals as a type who will keep on getting better and better; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: 2-2, winning those novices with something to spare; up in grade but an exciting prospect

runners.name: Della Pace
runners.spotlight: Her four juvenile starts yielded one win (1m AW maiden on third outing) and she was a clear second of four in a Class 4 handicap at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) on last month's reappearance; she can win more races but dramatic improvement is necessary if she's to take this.
runners.comment: Clear second in AW handicap on reappearance but dramatic improvement is necessary today

runners.name: K Sarra
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for comfortable win at Southwell (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start, then finished down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) last month on her reappearance; however, the drop back in trip looked very much against her and this sister to Pride Of Arras, who won last season's Dante over C&D for connections, could be an entirely different proposition over today's longer trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Down the field in Fred Darling; could be entirely different proposition at the longer trip
